title: Method of quantifying induced membrane permeability and of screening compounds able to prevent said permeability
enhanced_title: Method of quantifying induced changes of membrane permeability to various substances by detecting membrane channels made by misfolding peptides - useful in treatment of neurodegenerative diseases.
novelty: A method of quantifying induced changes of membrane permeability to various substances comprising the use of preparations of liposomes containing enclosed fluorescent probes which are measured by flow cytometry, is claimed, wherein the effect of treatments (chemical entities, biologically active molecules, and/or physical conditions) on peptide-induced changes of membrane permeability is estimated. It is claimed that chemical entities are intended to treat neurodegenerative diseases (including Alzheimer's disease, Parkinson's disease, Lou Gehrig's disease (amyotrophic lateral sclerosis), Huntington's disease, diabetes, diseases caused by prions, Down syndrome).
advantage: The method allows high-throughput testing of peptides.
biology: Experiments were conducted to measure effectiveness of various peptides to permeabilize membranes to calcium. After short incubation, calcium is added, and the mixture is analyzed on flow cytometer. Calcium ionophore ionomycin is used as a positive control, and a vehicle for peptide serves as a negative control. Results showed that unilamellar liposomes have the lowest ratio. In identified liposomes, the concentration of calcium is estimated. Liposomes without channels have low calcium, and corresponding low fluorescent signal of Fluo-4. Liposomes with channels have high calcium and intense fluorescence of Fluo-4. Also ratio of the number of liposomes with channels to total number of liposomes (or to the number of liposomes without channels) is the endpoint of test. Peptides which statistically significantly increase the ratio of permeabilized liposomes are considered channel-forming (example 1; page 23; figures 10-12).
examples: Method for creation of antibodies selectively affecting channels is described (example 5; page 24).
chemistry: Sequences provided in source document.

chemistry: Several compounds including butyl (R)-(1-(5-chloro-6-oxo-1,6-dihydropyridazin-4-yl)pyrrolidin-3-yl)carbamate (P-0001) (structure drawn from patent not from name) (page 135, table 1) are exemplified. title: 1,5-Dihydro-2,4-benzodiazepine-3-one derivative and application thereof

The compounds are stated to act on 5-HT 2A and 5-HT 2C receptors.
advantage: The compounds have superior or similar selectivity for 5-HT2A and better antipsychotic activity, while causing lower incidence of side effects such as sedation, deterioration in exercise capacity and cardiotoxicity, compared to pimavanserin.
biology: An in vivo test was performed to evaluate the effect of test compounds on head shaking behavior induced by (±)-1-(2,5-dimethoxy-4-iodophenyl)-2-aminopropane hydrochloride in mouse.The results showed that the test compounds including (I) (represented as NH-K-A19006), NH-K-A19001, NH-K-A19005 and NH-K-A19012 exhibited ED50 values of 0.012, 0.06, 0.15 and 0.30 mg/kg, respectively, whereas pimavanserin showed the value of 0.39 mg/kg, indicating better anti-psychiatric effect of the test compounds over pimavanserin. Also, (I) vs pimoserin showed ED50 values of 0.2648 vs 3.288 mg/kg in mice with MK-801-induced hyperactive behavior (example 23, pages 48-49; tables 3 and 4).
chemistry: Several compounds including 8-fluoro-2-(1-methylpiperidin-4-yl)-4-{[4-(cyclopropoxy)phenyl]methyl}-1,5-dihydro-2,4-benzodiazepine-3-one (I) (A19006, example 6, page 31; claim 10, page 58) are specifically claimed. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 53).

title: Method for evaluating neural action of test substance, and kit and nerve cell culture container therefor

A kit and a nerve cell culture vessel for evaluating nerve action of a test substance are claimed.
advantage: The method could evaluate the neural action of a test substance with high sensitivity, and have excellent quantitativeness and reproducibility especially in a low concentration range; and could measure activity of a small amount of a physiologically active substance.
biology: Experiments were performed to determine changes in drebrin cluster density due to glutamate between cultured neurons derived from the hippocampus of a healthy mouse and the hippocampus of an Alzheimer's disease model mouse using a COP plate. In cultured neurons derived from the hippocampus of healthy mice, a significant decrease in drebrin clusters was observed at a glutamate concentration of 30 ?M. In AD neurons, it was found that the linear density of drebrin clusters was consistently low from the wells to which glutamic acid was not added. Hence, it was possible to trace amounts of secretory proteins such as amyloid ? contribute to the phenotypic expression of cultured neurons derived from the hippocampus of Alzheimer's disease model mice (example 1, pages 18 and 19, figure 2).
chemistry: It is claimed that the visualization is performed by immunostaining with an anti-drebrin antibody.

title: Compositions and methods of promoting myelination

novelty: A method of increasing myelination of an axon, comprising contacting an oligodendrocyte progenitor cell (OPC) in the presence of an axon with an agent that inhibits GPR17 and/or agent that ablates and/or inhibits an activated microglia, or inhibits TNF? Receptor 2 or TNF?, thereby increasing myelination of the axon are claimed. A method of increasing OPC number and/or differentiation is also claimed. A method of treating a subject having a disease or injury associated with myelination failure is also claimed, where the subject has multiple sclerosis, leukodystrophy (eg adrenoleukodystrophy, alexander disease, canavan disease and Krabbe disease), Alzheimers disease, traumatic brain injury, spinal cord injury, or optic nerve injury. A composition and a method of identifying a compound that elicits differentiation of oligodendrocyte or an oligodendrocyte precursor cell is claimed. The PLX-3397 is known to acts as SL cytokine ligand inhibitor; CSF-1 antagonist; Flt3 tyrosine kinase inhibitor; Kit tyrosine kinase inhibitor, and Montelukast is known to acts as Leukotriene D4 antagonist.
advantage: The method increases the number of CC1 and/or Oligol positive OPCs.
biology: An in vivo study was performed to determine the effect of combinatorial treatment of (I) and (II) on robust myelination regenerated axons. The results showed that the combined treatment dramatically increased the numbers of CC1+ RTM+ double positive cells and the majority of these CC1+ cells had cytoplasmic Oligol, supporting the notion that this combinatorial treatment promoted both early and late differentiation of OPCs (example 5, pages 36, 37, figure 4A-4C).
chemistry: Six agents including PLX-3397 (I; page 18, claim 6, page 45), and montelukast (II; pages 20 and 21, claim 12, page 46) are specifically claimed for use.

advantage: The compounds are potent in their action, metabolically stable unlike BMS-204352, exhibit improved side effect profile, beneficial solubility and PK profile, allowing chronic administration.
biology: An in vitro whole-cell patch clamp assay was performed to assess the BK channel stimulatory activity of the test compounds in HEK-293 cell line expressing functional ? subunits of BK channels. The results showed that (I) exhibited a mean stimulation of BK channel activity at +50 mV of 204.09%, which effect was comparable to the effect (216.59%) of the highly potent but metabolically unstable BMS-204352. This potent stimulating effect, in combination with an improved side effect profile, renders (I) advantageous for use in therapy for fragile X syndrome. Also, (I) and a compound of example 6 at 50 ?M showed an effect of 61 and 89%, respectively, in HEK cells stably expressing human BK (known as KCa1.1 or KCNM1 isoform 1) channels (examples 41 and 42, pages 109 and 114; tables 1 and 2).

A method for determining the severity of a disease caused by synaptic dysfunction or a disease associated with synaptic dysfunction is claimed. It is claimed that the disease may be neurodegenerative disease, including Alzheimer's disease, corticobasal denuclear syndrome, Parkinson's disease, spinocerebellar degeneration and amyotrophic lateral sclerosis. It is claimed that the concentration of DARPs may be measured using an antibody that recognizes a drebrin A-specific epitope. An antibody for use in the method is claimed, which recognizes a drebrin A-specific epitope. A kit for determining the risk, presence/absence, or severity of onset risk, a disease caused by synaptic dysfunction, or a disease associated with synaptic dysfunction is claimed. Methods for screening a therapeutic agent for a disease associated with synaptic dysfunction using a synaptic dysfunction model (non-human animal or cultured cells) are claimed, comprising measuring the concentration of DARPs in the culture medium of the synaptic dysfunction model (non-human animal or cultured cells) or searching for dominant negative peptides against DARPs autoantibodies are claimed.
advantage: The methods are capable of early and easily determining the severity of a disease caused by synaptic dysfunction with high sensitivity.
biology: Cerebrospinal fluid sample was obtained from three patients with Alzheimer's disease (GHAD01-03, all mild dementia), one patient with amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS01), one patient with spinocerebellar degeneration (SCD01), and one patient with depression (SCD01). Many bands of DARPs were found in all the cerebrospinal fluid samples, and DARP40, DARP60, DARP70, DARP90, and DARP100 were confirmed as in the blood sample. The DARPs concentration was higher in two patients with AD (GHAD01 and 03), one patient with muscular ALS (ALS01), and one patient with spinocerebellar degeneration (SCD01), one patient with depression (Dep01). GHAD02 had a DARPs concentration similar to that of one depressed patient (Dep01). It was considered that the AD progression of GHAD02 has shifted to the chronic phase, the rapid leakage of DARPs as in the acute exacerbation phase has not occurred, and the DARPs concentration was low (example 3, pages 23 and 24, figure 6).

It is claimed that the method further comprises assaying whether the conformationally constrained peptide is able to inhibit association between the first and second candidate binding partner. A kit comprising a nucleic acid encoding the recombinant peptide, and the crosslinker, is finally claimed. advantage: The method allows to introduce an intra-molecular crosslink into a peptide to produce a conformationally constrained peptide whilst it remains inside the cell, is a 'one-pot reaction' method where the same cell can be used to produce the helix-constrained peptide and test for whether it is able to disrupt intracellular PPIs, and is more efficient process than the conventional methods where peptides are first chemically modified outside the cell to produce the conformationally constrained peptide and then subsequently introduced into a cell to confirm intracellular activity.

Methods for inhibiting, mitigating, delaying the onset of, or reducing the severity of one or more symptoms of HD or for reducing aggregation of Huntingtin protein or formation of Huntingtin fibrils in a subject in need thereof are claimed. A method of screening for a compound that binds to Huntingtin fibrils is claimed. A method of determining the presence of Huntingtin fibrils in a sample obtained from a subject is claimed. A method of purifying Huntingtin fibrils from a sample obtained from a subject is claimed.
advantage: Huntingtin-related peptide agents is efficacious against Huntingtins disease.
biology: Experiments were performed to assess whether HD peptides bind Httexl aggregates formed in tissues from two different Huntington mouse models. Results showed that, retinal sections from these mice incubated with 2 micromolar of HD8 conjugated with Alexa488 at its carboxyl terminus. Signal from HD8, in the form of small puncta, was observed mostly in the ganglion cell layer. HD8-HD1 -fluorescein (0.1 micromolar) was also used on retinal sections from 34-week-old R6/1 mice. Robust signal from the peptide could be seen in the ganglion cell layer, again in the form of small puncta. In addition, weaker signal from intranuclear aggregates was also observed. Further, the signal could now be detected in all layers of the retina where the nuclei reside. These results were consistent with the higher efficacy of multimerized HD peptides in binding Httexl aggregates. HD8-HD1 -fluorescein was also used on brain sections from R6/1 mice (34 weeks). Little signal from age-matched control mice, but abundant signal from R6/1 brain. Together, these results demonstrate the potential of HD peptides as biomarkers or therapeutic tools for Huntington disease (example 7, pages 33 and 34, figures 28 and 29).

novelty: New cyclobutyl amide compounds, their salts, isotopes, N-oxides, solvates, stereoisomers, compositions comprising them are claimed. The compounds are disclosed to act as MGL inhibitors.
advantage: No specific advantage is given.
biology: In an in vitro assay, (I) exhibited an IC50 value of 0.01 nM against MGL (pages 204-219; table 3)
chemistry: Several compounds including (7-((3,6-dimethyl-5-(trifluoromethyl)pyridin-2-yl)oxy)-2-azaspiro[3,5]nonan-2-yl)((1s,3s)-3-hydroxy-3-methylcyclobutyl)methanone (I) (compound 466, page 219, table 3; example 466, page 200; claim 19, page 234) are specifically claimed. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 220).

title: Advantageous tryptamine compositions for mental disorders or enhancement
enhanced_title: New tryptamine compounds are serotonin reuptake inhibitors and 5-HT2A agonists - useful for treating CNS diseases.
novelty: New tryptamine compounds, their salts, salts mixtures, enantiomers, racemates, enantiomerically enriched tryptamine compounds and their mixtures and compositions comprising them are claimed. The compounds are claimed to act as serotonin receptor enhancers, dopamine inhibitors, serotonin reuptake inhibitors, and 5-HT2A agonists, have entactogenic properties, useful for treating MDMA and CNS related diseases post-traumatic stress disorder, depression, dysthymia, anorexia, generalized anxiety, drug abuse, memory loss, attention deficit hyperactivity disorder, autism and panic disorder. The uses of a tryptamine compounds, their salts, salts mixtures, enantiomers, racemates, enantiomerically enriched tryptamine compounds for treating the diseases are further claimed. The compounds are disclosed to act as MAO-A inhibitors and dopamine transporter agonist.
advantage: The compounds have good efficacy with reduced adverse effects.
biology: A clinical trial protocol is presented to estimate the efficacy of the test compounds on entactogenic effect of authenticity. However, no results are presented (example 11, pages 220-222). Further in an in vivo experiment, (I) showed dose dependent reduction on dopaminergic and noradrenergic effects compared to MDMA in MDMA trained rats. In another in vitro assay, HCL salt of (I) exhibited an IC50 value of 3.5 uM against hSERT (example 6, pages 211 and 212; table 8).
chemistry: Several compounds including 1-(5-fluoro-1H-indol-3-yl)-2-(methylamino)propan-1-one (BK-5F-NM-AMT; I) (example 1, page 173; claim 1, page 225) are specifically claimed. Markush structures are presented (claim 5, page 226).

title: NLRX1 Ligands
enhanced_title: New quinazolinones are NLRX1 agonists - useful for treating, eg allergic disease, cancer, multiple sclerosis, asthma, Alzheimer's disease and an infectious disease.
novelty: Novel quinazolinone derivatives, their salts and esters, compositions comprising them and their use for treating, eg autoimmune disease, an allergic disease, cancer, multiple sclerosis, asthma, Alzheimer's disease, Parkinson's disease, neuroinflammation, inflammatory bowel disease and an infectious disease, are claimed. The compounds are disclosed to be NLRX1 agonists.
advantage: No specific advantage is given.
biology: In an in vivo test, C57BL6 mice were challenged at 6- to 8-weeks of age with MOG immunization (Mycobacterium tuberculosis (H37RA)). Mice were treated daily with (I) at 20 mg/kg via oral gavage. (I) reduced the disease activity index of mice challenged with MOG emulsion and accelerated recovery from neurological deficiencies. In spinal cord, oral dose of (I) reduced expression of Tnf and Il1b, suggesting an ability to reduce inflammation in the central nervous system (example 9, pages 45 and 46; figures 5 and 6).
chemistry: Several compounds are specifically claimed, eg (2-((3-(3,4-dihydro-2H-pyran-6-yl)-1,2,4-oxadiazol-5-yl)methyl)-6-(6-hydroxypyridin-3-yl)quinazolin-4(1H)-one) (NX-64-3; I) (page 35; figure 2B; claim 27, page 60). A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 57).

title: Modified imidazopyridines as glucosylceramide synthase inhibitors
enhanced_title: New modified imidazopyridines are glucosylceramide synthase inhibitors - useful for treating lysosomal storage diseases, neurodegenerative disease and cancers.
novelty: New modified imidazopyridine compounds, their salts, composition comprising them and thier use for treatment of disease associated with abnormal or overexpression of GCS disrupts ceramide-induced apoptosis or treatment of lysosomal storage diseases, kidney disease, neurodegenerative diseases (eg dementia with Lewy bodies, Parkinson's Disease), diabetes mellitus, obesity, leukemia, polycystic kidney disease, hyperinsulemia and thyroid carcinomas, are claimed. The composition further comprising one or more additional therapeutic agents, is also claimed.
advantage: The compounds are safe and effective.
biology: An in vitro UDP-Glo™ glucosylceramide synthase biochemical assay was performed to evaluate the effect of test compounds on the activity of endogenous levels of GCS enzyme using human A375 malignant melanoma skin cells. (I) showed GCS EC50 (nM) value of 0.029 (page 82-86, table 3).
chemistry: Several compounds including N-(pyridin-3-yl)-8-(2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)phenyl)-6-(trifluoromethyl)imidazo[1,2-a]pyridine-2-carboxamide (I) (given as compound 15, table 1, page 59; claim 13, page 93). A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 87).

title: Solid state forms of SEP-363856 and process for preparation thereof
enhanced_title: New crystalline forms of SEP-363856 salts eg fumarate salt - useful for treating schizophrenia and Parkinson's disease psychosis.
novelty: New crystalline forms of SEP-363856 salts, such as hydrobromide and fumarate (designated as forms 1 and F1, respectively), processes for their preparation and compositions comprising them are claimed. Also claimed is their use for treating schizophrenia and Parkinson's disease psychosis. SEP-363856 is disclosed to be an agonist for the trace amine-associated receptor 1 and for serotonin-1A receptor.
advantage: No specific advantage is given.
biology: No biological data are presented
examples: Methanol (0.6 mL) was added to (I) free base (100 mg) and magnetically stirred at RT for 15 min, and fumaric acid (70 mg dissolved in 1 ml methanol) was added, stirred and cooled to 4 °C and an anti-solvent ie methyl tertiary-butyl ether (0.6 mL) was added dropwise and precipitated. The slurry was magnetically stirred at 4 °C for 72 h and filtered by Buchner and dried in vacuum oven at 40 °C for 18 h to obtain crystalline form F1 of (I) (example 2, pages 15-16; figure 2).
chemistry: Two salts of SEP-363856, including SEP-363856 fumarate (I) (page 1; claim 3, page 18) are specifically claimed.

title: Compounds, targets and pathways for macrophage modulation
enhanced_title: Use of macrophage activation modulators - for treating cancers, fibrosis, infectious disease, inflammatory disease, metabolic disease, cardiovascular disease, an autoimmune disease and neurodegenerative disease.
novelty: A method of identifying a modulator of macrophage activation, comprising contacting a primary macrophage cell with a candidate agent, monitoring or photographing; and optionally comparing the cell's morphology in the presence and absence of the candidate agent; wherein a change in morphology in the presence of the candidate agent is indicative of modulation of macrophage activation is claimed. Also claimed is a method of treating cancer (eg acute nonlymphocytic leukemia, primary brain tumors or stomach cancer), fibrosis, or an infectious disease (eg COVID-19), inflammatory disease (eg lupus nephritis), metabolic disease (eg diabetes), cardiovascular disease, an autoimmune disease (eg rheumatoid arthritis), or a neurodegenerative disease (eg Alzheimer's disease) using the modulator of macrophage activation (serotonin, histamine and dopamine transporter or receptor, an adrenoceptor, VEGF, EGF and/or leptin) eg di phenyl eneiodonium. It is finally claimed that the treatment further comprising second cancer therapy
advantage: No specific advantage is given.
biology: An in vivo study was performed to evaluate the effect of (I) on glycolysis and inflammatory responses of Kupffer cells in HFD-fed mice. The results showed that the GSEA and functional enrichment analysis showed that upregulation of genes associated with immune and inflammatory responses in KCs from mice fed with high fat diet (HFD) or normal diet (ND) (Figures 25A-25C). Expression of genes involved in inflammation were significantly suppressed in KCs from HFD mice following (I) treatment. In contrast, expression of many other genes that was down-regulated in KCs from HFD mice were significantly upregulated after (I) treatment (Figure 25A). Interestingly, expression of genes involved in glycolysis, oxidative phosphorylation and fatty acid metabolism was downregulated in KCs of HFD mice, whereas expression of these genes was upregulated in KCs from HFD mice after (I) treatment. Macrophage polarization index (MPI) analysis showed that KCs were polarized to Ml in HFD-fed mice but to M2 in mice on normal diet, while KCs were reprogrammed to an intermediated phenotype in (I)-treated HFD mice (example 4, page 103, figures 25A-25D).
chemistry: Several macrophage activation modulators eg diphenyleneiodonium (I) (claim 94, page 122) are specifically claimed for use.

title: Amine derivative
enhanced_title: New amide derivatives are DYRK1A, DYRK1B, DYRK2, and DYRK3 inhibitors - useful for treating traumatic brain injury, Alzheimer's disease and brain tumor.
novelty: New amide derivatives, their salts, compositions comprising them, are claimed. The compounds are disclosed to act as DYRK1?, DYRK1?, DYRK2, and DYRK3 inhibitors.
advantage: The compounds have strong DYRK inhibitory activity, and safety.
biology: An in vivo experiment was carried out to estimate the safety of the test compounds in B6C3F1 mice. The compounds exhibit good safety. However, no results were presented (test example 2, pages 187 and 188). Further in an in vitro assay, (I) exhibited IC50 values of ? 0.01 ?M against DYRK1A and DYRK1B, and 0.01 to 0.1 ?M against DYRK2 and DYRK3 (test example 1, pages 181-187; table 3).
chemistry: Several compounds including (3aR,6R,7aR)-1-(2H-[1,3]dioxolo[4,5-e][1,3]benzothiazole-7-yl]-6-(fluoromethyl)hexahydropyrano[3,4-d]imidazole-2(3H)-one (I) (example 140, pages 47 and 172; claim 17, page 202) are specifically claimed. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 189).

title: Cyclic urea derivative

novelty: Novel cyclic urea derivatives, their salts, compositions, and their use for treating disease associated with DYRK including Alzheimer's disease, Parkinson's disease, brain tumor, lung cancer, sickle red erythrocyte anemia, chronic renal disease, osteoporosis, progressive supranuclear palsy, traumatic brain injury and mild cognitive disorder, are claimed. title: Casein kinase 1 delta modulators

advantage: The compounds are effective in their action.
biology: An in vitro test was performed to evaluate the CSNK1D kinase activity of test compounds living Chinese Hamster Ovary (CHO) cells. (I) displayed a CSNK1D BRET IC50 (µM) and ADP-GloTM CSNK1D AC50 (µM) values were found to be 0.032 and 0.002 respectively (page 213-218).
chemistry: Several compounds including 4-[3-(5-fluoro-2-pyridyl)-1-methyl-pyrazol-4-yl]-1H-pyrrolo[2,3-b]pyridin-6-amine (I) (example 119, page 191-192; claim 22, page 228) are specifically claimed. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 219).

novelty: New substituted pyrazole derivatives, their salts, isotopes, N-oxides, solvates, and stereoisomers thereof, composition comprising them and method of their use for treating a subject suffering from or diagnosed with a disease, disorder, or condition mediated by CSNK1D selected from neurodegenerative diseases eg type 1 bipolar depression, type 2 bipolar depression, major depressive disorder, familial advanced sleep phase syndrome, Alzheimer's disease, Parkinson's disease, and amyotrophic lateral sclerosis psychiatric disorder, are claimed. chemistry: Several compounds including 2-(4-fluorophenyl)-6,6-dimethyl-3-(1H-pyrazolo[3,4-b]pyridin-4-yl)-6,7-dihydro-5H-pyrazolo[5,1-b][1,3]oxazine (I) (example 212, pages 535 and 536; claim 24, page 794) are specifically claimed. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 773).

The compounds disclosed to act as RIPK2 and c-ABL kinase inhibitors.
advantage: No specific advantage is given.
biology: An in vitro test was performed to determine the kinsase activity and IL-6 secretion in BV-2 cell. The IC50 (nM) values for (I) were found to be 1-100 nM for RIP2 kinase and c-Abl Kinase and 101 -500 nM for IL-6 respectively (table 2, page 71-76).

title: Histone deacetylase inhibitors and uses thereof
enhanced_title: Use of histone deacetylase inhibitor - for treating autism spectrum disorder and/or intellectual disability.
novelty: The use of histone deacetylase inhibitor, for treating autism spectrum disorder and/or intellectual disability, is claimed. A composition comprising histone deacetylase inhibitor and carrier for treating the same, is also claimed. Methods to identify a subject to be treated with and to monitor the efficacy of a histone deacetylase inhibitor for treating the same, comprising measuring the level of GTF2I in a biological sample of the subject and comparing the measured level to a control level, are further claimed.
advantage: The compounds act as potent and selective inhibitors of HDAC1, HDAC2, HDAC3 and/or HDAC6 inhibitor over HDACs, exhibits chemical diversity, pharmacokinetic properties and blood-brain barrier (BBB) penetration ability.
biology: In vitro studies were conducted to test effects of HDAC inhibitors on cell viability and lower protein levels in iPSC-derived NGN2-induced neurons of GTF2I in 7Dup cortical organoids. The results showed that treatment with (I), mocetinostat and RG-2388 at 0.1 and 1 ?M had no significant effect on cell viability. (I) and mocetinostat at highest concentration induced a 20-30% reduction of cell viability and have raised apoptosis as confirmed by Cleaved-PARP accumulation. Also, effect on GTF2I level was confirmed as dose dependent in four different patient-derived iN lines (example 4, pages 23-24; figure 8).
chemistry: Several inhibitors including vorinostat (I) (claim 7, page 32) are specifically claimed for use.

biology: In an in vitro assay, (I) exhibited an IC50 value of 0.004 ?M against THP-1 cells (example 4, pages 108-111).
chemistry: Several compounds including compound 6 (I) (page 77; claim 21, page 124) are specifically claimed. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 114).

title: Substituted pyridine derivatives as SARM1 inhibitors
enhanced_title: Substituted pyridine compounds are SARM1 inhibitors - useful for treating neurological diseases.

A method of inhibiting SARM1 comprising contacting the SARM1 with a compound and a method of inhibiting axonal degeneration is claimed, wherein the axonal degeneration is caused by abnormal reduction or depletion of NAD+ in the axons. A method of treating or preventing a neurological disorder, is a neurodegenerative disease, such as spinal muscular atrophy, chemotherapy induced peripheral neuropathy, multiple sclerosis, traumatic brain injury, spinal cord injury, stroke, Parkinson' disease, glaucoma, Huntington's disease, and Alzheimer's disease.
advantage: No specific advantage is given.
biology: An in vivo protocol test was performed to determine the effect of SARM1 inhibitors in Chemotherapy-induced Peripheral Neuropathy (CIPN) mice model. However no resultant data are presented (example C, page 139). Also an in vitro test was performed to determine the effect of (I) in SARM1 cells. (I) showed an IC50 value of <1 uM (example A, table 1; pages 134-135).
chemistry: Several substituted pyridine compounds are specifically claimed, including (rac)-N-(1-(1-(pyridin-4-yl)ethyl)-1H-pyrazol-3-yl)thiazol-2-amine (example 1, pages 42 and 43) (I; claim 40, page 150). A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 142).

title: Novel prymidodiazepine derivative and use thereof
enhanced_title: New prymidodiazepine derivatives are tau protein aggregation and PERK signaling inhibitors - useful for treating tauopathies.
novelty: New pyrimidodiazepine derivatives or their salts, compositions comprising them, that act as inhibitors of PERK signaling activation, useful for treating tauopathies such as Alzheimer's disease, frontotemporal dementia, progressive supranuclear palsy, traumatic brain injury, Pick's disease, Chronic traumatic encephalopathy, Parkinson's disease and Huntington's disease. Also claimed is a method for preventing or treating tauopathy, by administering the compounds.
advantage: The compounds inhibit tau aggregation by acting on a pathway in PERK signal transduction.
biology: An in vivo test was conducted in Alzheimer's mouse model, to determine the PK studies (I). The values of AUC and Cmax for (I) Tmax of 1.67h were found to be 5.65?g.h/mL and 0.857?g/mL, respectively (test example 6, page 59, table 2, figure 27).
chemistry: Several compounds including SB-1617 (I) (compound 117, pages 42 and 43; claim 8, page 68) are specifically claimed. Sequences provided in source document. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 62)

title: 3-Hydroxyoxindole derivatives as CRHR2 antagonist
enhanced_title: New 3-hydroxyoxindole derivatives are CRHR2 antagonists - useful for treating gastrointestinal disorders, major depressive disorders, pain, inflammation, fertility problems and heart disease.
novelty: New 3-hydroxyoxindole derivatives or its salts or prodrug, and composition comprising them and its preparation method is claimed. advantage: The compounds showed an excellent CRHR2 antagonistic activities and have a number of therapeutic applications.
biology: An in vivo protocol was performed to determine the effect of CRHR2 agonist in male C57BL/6 mice. However no resultant data are presented. An in vitro test was performed to determine the effect of (I) against CRHR2. (I) showed an IC50 value of < 0.2 microM (page 153).
chemistry: Several 3-hydroxyoxindole derivatives are specifically claimed, eg 5-chloro-2-(difluoromethyl)-N-((1r,4r)-4-((3-hydroxy-3-(4-methoxyphenyl)-2-oxoindolin-1-yl)methyl)cyclohexyl)nicotinamide (example 2; table 1-1, page 120) (I) (claim 7, page 162). A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 157).

title: Compounds and compositions as modulators of TLR signaling
enhanced_title: New compounds and compositions are modulators of TLR signaling - useful for treating neurological disorders.
novelty: New heterocyclic compounds their salts, compositions comprising them, their use as toll-like receptors (TLRs) for the treatment of inflammatory diseases (psoriasis) and neurological disorders (Alzheimer's disease, Parkinson's disease, dementia with Lewy bodies, multiple system atrophy, amyotrophic lateral sclerosis, Huntington's disease and multiple sclerosis. A method for interfering with the heterodimerization of TLR2 by modulating, preventing, slowing, reversing, or inhibiting TLR2 heterodimerization in a cell by contacting the cell with an effective amount of the compound is also claimed. A method for inhibiting TLR9 in a cell by contacting the cell with an effective amount of the compound is finally claimed.
advantage: Compounds are potent in their action
biology: An in vitro test was conducted in TLR9 assay, to determine the cytotoxicity of (I). (I) showed an IC 50 value of 0.4, 0.3 and 0.6 against Pam2CSK4, Pam3CSK4 and ODN2006 respectively (biological example 2, page 184-186, table A)
chemistry: Several heterocyclic compounds including 3-fluoro-5-formyl-4-hydroxy-N-(6-(pyrrolidin-1-yl)-5-(trifluoromethyl)pyridin-3-yl)benzamide (I) (compound 19) (table 1, page 44; claim 39, page 206) are specifically claimed. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 188).

title: Use of HDAC6 as preventive or therapeutic agent for TDP-43 proteinopathy
enhanced_title: Use of histone deacetylase 6 (HDAC6) protein or a polynucleotide encoding the same - for treating TDP-43 proteinopathy, such as Parkinson's disease.
novelty: Use of a pharmaceutical composition, comprising HDAC6 (Histone Deacetylase 6) protein or a polynucleotide encoding the same as an active ingredient for preventing or treating TDP-43 proteinopathy (eg amyotrophic lateral sclerosis, Parkinson disease or Huntington's disease). A method for screening a drug for preventing or treating TDP-43 proteopathies is claimed, comprising contacting the sample to be analyzed with cells containing an expression vector containing HDAC6 protein or a polynucleotide encoding the same under conditions in which the ubiquitin proteasome system (UPS) is damaged, measuring the expression level or activity of the HDAC6, and when it is measured that the expression level or activity of the HDAC6 is increased, determining the sample as a drug capable of preventing or treating TDP-43 proteinopathy. Use of pharmaceutical composition for the prevention or treatment of mitochondrial disorders encephalopathy as a mediator is claimed.
advantage: No specific advantage is given.
biology: Experiments were performed to analysis the effect of overexpression of HDAC6 on the improvement of UPS damage and behavioral defects in the Drosophila model of TDP-43 proteopathies. The TDP-43 proteinopathy-induced Drosophila showed a decreased survival level within 7 days of analysis, and most of the Drosophila died from the 15th day. On the other hand, in the group induced overexpression of HDAC6, neurotoxicity by TDP-43/ATXN2-32Q was suppressed, and the lifespan of fruit flies was increased. These results indicate that neurotoxicity by TDP-43/ATXN2 can be inhibited by HDAC6 expression, and HDAC6 expression also reduces polyubiquitinated aggregates in the brain tissue of TDP-43/ATXN2-expressing Drosophila. It was found that it can improve the UPS damage caused by TDP-43/ATXN2 (example 4, pages 17 and 18, figure 6).
chemistry: Sequences provided in source document.

title: Neurite outgrowth promoter
enhanced_title: Use of a neurite outgrowth promoter, eg rebamipide - for treating autism spectrum disorders.
novelty: The use of a neurite outgrowth promoter, such as rebamipide, sotalol and rifabutin their derivatives or salts for treating autism spectrum disorders, such as Dup15q syndrome, Fragile X syndrome, Rett syndrome, nodular sclerosis, Costello syndrome and Clefstra syndrome is claimed. Also claimed is a method for screening for a therapeutic agent for autism spectrum disorder by contacting a test substance with a nerve cell induced to differentiate from an artificial pluripotent stem cell derived from an autism spectrum disorder patient and measuring the expression level of one or more genes selected from the group consisting of RNT4, SEMA6A and DCC in a nerve cell and the UBE3A gene. It is stated that the compounds inhibit the expression levels of RNT4, SEMA6A, DCC and UBE3A .
advantage: No specific advantage is given.
biology: An in vitro study was conducted to evaluate activity of (I) on gene expression of neurite growth inhibitory molecules. Results showed that (I) alone suppressed expression of DCC mRNA, but not sotalol and rifabutin (page 22, figure 5F, page 22).
chemistry: Three compounds including rebamipide (I) (claim 1, page 23) are specifically claimed for use. Sequence information is provided in the source document (table 2, page 15).

title: Cyclic isothiourea derivatives as CXCR4 modulators
enhanced_title: New cyclic isothiourea derivatives are CXCR4 modulators - useful for treating inflammatory disorders, autoimmune disorders and autoinflammatory disorders.
novelty: New cyclic isothiourea derivatives, composition comprising them is claimed. The compounds are claimed to be useful in the treatment or prevention of an inflammatory disorder, an autoimmune disorder, an autoinflammatory disorder, or an interferonopathy, such as type II diabetes, autism, rheumatoid arthritis, multiple sclerosis, Alzheimer's disease, juvenile dermatomyositis, and an inflammatory complication associated with a viral infection. In vitro use of a compound as a CXCR4 modulator, is finally claimed.
advantage: The compounds are potent inhibitors of the production of interferons and inflammatory cytokines by specifically targeting the CXCR4 minor pocket.
biology: An in vivo test was performed to determine the immunomodulating effect (decreased type 1 IFNs) of dihydrochloride salt of (I) in acute inflammation model Male 129S8 mice. When administering (I) once i.n. at a dose of 450 µg, the observed concentrations of all type 1 IFNs are significantly decreased as well, but even lower than the concentrations observed after ibuprofen treatment (Figures 8A to 8C, page 93).
chemistry: Several cyclic isothiourea derivatives are specifically claimed, including 6-chloro-2-((4-(pyrrolidin-1-yl)butyl)thio)-1,4-dihydroquinazoline (example 60, page 166) (I) (claim 12, page 270). A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 235).

title: Aroyl substituted tricyclic compound, preparation method therefor and use thereof
enhanced_title: New aroyl substituted tricyclic compounds are BTK inhibitors - useful for treating cancer and xenoimmune, autoimmune and inflammatory diseases.
novelty: New aroyl substituted tricyclic compounds, their salts, stereoisomers, solvates, prodrugs and/or deuterated compounds and compositions comprising them are claimed. Also claimed is their use for treating diseases associated with BTK and/or with abnormal B cell activation such as cancer and xenoimmune, autoimmune and inflammatory diseases (eg myasthenia gravis, multiple sclerosis, psoriasis, diabetes, Sjogren's disease, atherosclerosis, Parkinson's disease, Alzheimer's disease or septic shock). The compounds are disclosed to act as Bruton's tyrosine kinase (BTK) inhibitors.
advantage: The compounds have high activity, good selectivity and low toxicity and side effects.
biology: In an in vitro p-BTK cell experiment, (I) showed an IC50 value (in nM) of 2.5 and 1 against phosphorylation level of BTK (wild type) or BTK(C481S), respectively in HEK293 cells (test example 2, pages 230-232; table 2).
chemistry: Several compounds, including 9-(2-chlorobenzoyl)-2,2-dimethyl-1,2,4,7-tetrahydro-3H-pyrrolo[3',2' : 5,6]pyrido[3,4-b]pyrazin-3-one (Z120; I) (example 169, pages 196 and 197; claim 10, page 250) are specifically claimed. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 235)..

title: Method for differentiating pluripotent stem cells into dopaminergic nerve cells in midbrain substantia nigra
enhanced_title: Method for differentiating pluripotent stem cells into dopaminergic nerve cells in the midbrain substantia nigra - useful for treating neurodegenerative diseases.
novelty: A method for preparing midbrain substantia nigra dopaminergic neurons is claimed, comprising (1) placing stem cells in a culture medium containing a neural inducer, and adding components separately in multiple stages; and (2) obtaining the stem cell-derived substantia nigra dopaminergic neurons from the culture. A midbrain substantia nigra dopaminergic nerve cell prepared by the method is claimed. A midbrain substantia nigra dopaminergic neuron expresses surface molecular markers of midbrain substantia nigra dopaminergic neurons, including tyrosine hydroxylase (TH), FOXA2, EN1, LMX1A, NURR1 and/or GIRK2 is claimed. The application of the substantia nigra dopaminergic neurons in the preparation of a preparation for treating neurodegenerative diseases (Parkinson's disease (PD), Alzheimer's disease, dementia with Lewy bodies, Huntington's disease, amyotrophic lateral sclerosis, and nerve damage) is claimed. A preparation for treating neurodegenerative diseases comprising the substantia nigra dopaminergic neurons is claimed, wherein the preparation is further used for as a graft for transplantation of substantia nigra or striatum and prevention and treatment of motor dysfunction. A method for screening a substance for improving neurodegenerative diseases is claimed, comprising treating a model system with a candidate substance.
advantage: The method is highly efficient in differentiating pluripotent stem cells for the enrichment of dopaminergic neurons in the substantia nigra.
biology: The functional effects of substantia nigra transplanted cells were assessed by amphetamine-induced rotation, rotation test and cylinder test before and every 4 weeks after transplantation. PD mice transplanted with striatal cells began to recover from amphetamine-induced rotation at 3 months and 4 months after transplantation. Over time, mice gradually showed hypercompensation by rotating to the contralateral side. After 4-5 months, functional recovery was also observed in PD mice transplanted with mDA in the substantia nigra; however, overcompensation disappeared by 6 months (p < 0.001). In contrast, those mice that received glutamate neurons or artificial cerebrospinal fluid (ACSF; control) in the substantia nigra did not show any signs of recovery in amphetamine-induced rotation (p > 0.05) (example 6, page 24, figure 6).

biology: Studies were performed to test ENO1down and ENO1up mutants for their ability to rescue glycolysis (lactate accumulation in the medium) in HeLa cells after knock-down of the endogenous ENO1. The data indicates that ENO1wt rescued lactate production. Of note, the RNA binding-deficient mutant ENO1down was as active as the wild-type protein, showing that the K343A mutation does not incapacitate the enzyme. In contrast, ENO1up failed to rescue the knock-down-induced inhibition of lactate accumulation, although it was fully active when tested in the absence of RNA in vitro. The activity measurements were controlled with a mutant lacking enzymatic activity (ENO1as, E295A/D320A/K394A. These results were consistent with the notion that ENO1's RNA binding interferes with its enzymatic activity in cells. Also, the results unambiguously confirmed specific riboregulation of lactate production by the ENO1 target RNAs tested (example 2, page 40; figures 8-9).

biology: Cortical organoids were produced, and analyzed for three genes (SUV420H1, ARID1B, and CHD8) linked to macrocephaly and/or microcephaly in patients. For SUV420H1, mutant organoids from all genomic contexts were significantly larger than controls at both two weeks (17-18 d. z.v.) and one month (27-30 d.i.v.), reminiscent of the macrocephalic phenotype observed in individuals carrying de novo mutations in the SUV420H1 gene. For ARID1B, haploinsufficient organoids showed an increase in size in the Mito210 background at both two weeks and one month, but an opposite effect in the Mito294 background. For CHD8, at one month (24-25 d.i.v.), mutant organoids were significantly larger compared to controls in two out of the four lines tested, across multiple batches. These data indicate that human cortical organoids bearing haploinsufficient mutations in SUV420H1, ARID1B, and CHD8 replicate size defects related to the macrocephalic and microcephalic abnormalities seen in patients carrying these mutations (page 75, figures 13A 13B, 20G-20J, and 30).

novelty: A method for preparing a brain organoid neuron spheroid construct comprising a co-culturing step of co-culturing brain organoids and neuronal spheroids is claimed, wherein the brain organoid is a cerebral organoid and the neuron spheroid is a motor neuron spheroid. A brain organoid-neuron spheroid construct comprising a brain organoid, and neuronal spheroids bound to the underside of the brain organoid is claimed. A drug screening method comprising the step of treating a target drug to a brain organoid-neuron spheroid construct comprising a brain organoid, and neuronal spheroids bound to the bottom of the brain organoid is claimed, wherein the target drug is a candidate substance for the prevention or treatment of brain diseases causing movement disorders including Parkinson's disease or Huntington's disease.
advantage: No specific advantage is given.
biology: Studies were conducted for confirmation of changes in bioelectrical signals of cerebral organoid-motoneuron spheroid structures on treating with (I), a cranial nerve stimulant. The cerebral organoids had electrical signals amplified by (I), but there was no change in the motor neuron spheroids. Also, when the cerebral organoid-motoneuron spheroid structure was treated with (I), the electrical signal amplification measured in the motor neuron spheroid in contact with the substrate was connected to the neural network between the two tissues, so that the excitation of the cerebral organoid was affected (example 6, page 9, figure 4).
examples: Methods for preparation of cerebral organoids from human induced pluripotent stem cells, preparation of motor neuron spheroids from human neural stem cells and induction of cerebral organoid-motoneuron spheroid binding are described (examples 1-5, pages 6-8).
chemistry: Caffeine is (I) (page 5).

title: N-Containing heteroaryl derivative, and pharmaceutical composition for preventing or treating protein kinase-related diseases comprising same as active ingredient
enhanced_title: Novel N-containing heteroaryls as AURKA, BTK, HPK1, CDK7 and LRRK2 inhibitors - useful for treating, eg cancer, Alzheimer's disease, multiple sclerosis, inflammatory disease and allergy.
novelty: Novel N-containing heteroaryl derivatives, their salts, isomers, solvates and hydrates, compositions comprising them, process for their preparation, and their use as AURKA, BTK, HPK1, CDK7 and LRRK2 inhibitors, for treating, eg cancer, degenerative brain disease, Alzheimer's disease, Parkinson's disease, multiple sclerosis, amyotrophic lateral sclerosis, stroke, inflammatory disease, eg dermatitis, and allergy, are claimed.
advantage: No specific advantage is given.
biology: In an in vitro test, compounds were incubated with human HPK1 or LRRK2. (I) showed an IC50 value of 0.05 ?M or less (experimental example 1 and 2, pages 35-38; table 2).
chemistry: Several compounds, including N-(5-cyclopropyl-1H-pyrazol-3-yl)-2-(6-(6-((2-methoxypyrimidin-5-yl)methyl)-3,6-diazabicyclo[3.1.1]heptan-3-yl)pyridin-3-yl)quinazolin-4-amine (I) (compound 3; table 1, example 3, page 24; claim 5, page 45) are specifically claimed. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 43).

title: Fusion proteins comprising an e2 ubiquitin or ubiquitin-like conjugating domain and a targeting domain for specific protein degradation
enhanced_title: Fusion proteins comprising E2 ubiquitin or ubiquitin-like conjugating domain - useful for treating disease such as cancer, diabetes, autoimmune disease, and Alzheimer's disease.
novelty: A molecule comprising a regulation domain comprising an E2 ubiquitin or ubiquitin-like conjugating domain, and a targeting domain capable of targeting the regulation domain to a substrate is claimed. A compound comprising a molecule and targeting moiety is claimed. A polynucleotide encoding molecule or compound, vector comprising polynucleotide, host cell comprising polynucleotide or vector are claimed. A composition comprising a molecule, compound, polynucleotide, vector or host cell, and a further therapeutic agent is claimed. A pharmaceutical composition comprising molecule, compound, polynucleotide, vector, host cell, or composition, and carrier, diluent or excipient is claimed. A method of delivering molecule to a cell in individual, is claimed. A kit of parts comprising regulation domain is claimed. A method of preventing or treating a disease or condition mediated by an aberrant level of a substrate or form thereof in a subject, by comprising administering the molecule, compound, polynucleotide, vector, cell, pharmaceutical composition, or the composition to the subject is claimed. Methods of regulating substrate, identifying substrate as potential drug target, and assessing function of a substrate, and identifying test agent that may be useful in preventing or treating disease or condition are claimed. The use of molecule or the compound in drug target validation or in drug discovery is claimed. It is also claimed that the disease or condition is cancer, diabetes, autoimmune disease, Alzheimer's disease, Parkinson's disease, pain, viral disease, bacterial disease, prionic disease, fungal disease, parasitic disease, arthritis, or inflammatory disease.
advantage: The molecule has improved in vivo metabolic stability, and cellular permeability.
biology: Experiment was performed to determine whether the E2 fusion polypeptide format could be successful in degrading a predominantly nuclear target. The results show that HuR protein expression was reduced, relative to control levels, in MDA-MD-231 and U20S cell lines expressing HA_UBE2D1_Linker 2_HuR17, HA_UBE2D1_Linker 2_HuR8, HA_HuR17_Linker 2_UBE2D1 and HA_HuR8_Linker 2_UBE2D1 fusion proteins. Further, the HuR levels were reduced by as much as 90% compared to control levels observed for cells expressing HA_UBE2D1_Linker 2_Cas9 and HA_Cas9_Linker 2_UBE2D1 (example 7, page 80-81, figure 11 and 12).
chemistry: Sequences provided in source document.

title: Resorcinol compounds, preparation method therefor and use thereof in nervous system diseases
enhanced_title: New resorcinol compounds are CB1 and CB2 modulators - useful for treating central nervous system diseases.
novelty: New resorcinol compounds, or its enantiomers, diastereomers, racemates, and its inorganic or organic salts, crystalline hydrates and solvates, and composition comprising them and its preparation method are claimed. The compounds are claimed to be useful for treating, alleviating and/or preventing central nervous system diseases, such as epilepsy, schizophrenia, affective disorder, Parkinson's disease, Huntington's disease, Alzheimer's disease, Bipolar I Disorder, ADHD, drug addiction. Also claimed a composition comprising resorcinol compounds, other drugs for the treatment of central nervous system diseases optionally selected from the group consisting of antipsychotics drugs, antiepileptic drugs or antidepressant drugs; pharmaceutically acceptable carriers or excipients.
advantage: The compounds have improved oral bioavailability and stronger efficacy for the treatment of various central nervous system diseases.
biology: An in vivo test was performed to determine the antidepressant effect of (I) in Male ICR mice using forced swim test. (I) showed significant antidepressant-like effects at a low dose of 3 mg/kg, while CBD only showed significant antidepressant-like effects at a dose of 30 mpk (page 39).
chemistry: Several resorcinol compounds are specifically claimed, eg compound 1 (I) (example 1, page 17; claim 3, page 46). A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 45).

title: N-Linked isoquinoline amides as LRRK2 inhibitors, pharmaceutical compositions, and uses thereof
enhanced_title: New N-linked isoquinoline amides are LRRK2 inhibitors - useful for treating eg Parkinson's disease, Lewy body dementia, and Alzheimer's disease.

The compounds are disclosed to act as LRRK2 inhibitors.

Several compounds, including (I) (presumed to be compound example 5.17) showed an pIC50 value of (I) 10.09 against LRRK2 (pages 159-165; table 12).

title: Process of reworking
enhanced_title: Novel crystalline forms of iclepertin and their preparation - useful for treating Alzheimer's disease, Schizophrenia, and cognitive disorder.
novelty: A process for reworking a crystalline iclepertin comprising the steps of drying the crystalline iclepertin in a tray dryer or in a pan dryer to provide a dry form of crystalline iclepertin, followed by homogenizing the dry form of crystalline iclepertin to provide a reworked form of crystalline iclepertin is claimed. Also claimed is a homogenized and dry form of crystalline iclepertin prepared by a process comprising the steps of drying a crystalline iclepertin in a tray dryer or in a pan dryer to produce a dry crystalline form of iclepertin and homogenizing the dry crystalline form of iclepertin to provide the homogenized and dry form of crystalline iclepertin. Further claimed is a crystalline iclepertin (having bulk density of ?0.20 g/mL, or ?0.15 g/mL, or ?0.15 g/mL, or ?0.10 g/mL) and compositions comprising them. A composition comprising 2 to 8% by weight of iclepertin, 50 to 70% lactose monohydrate, 20 to 30% microcrystalline cellulose, 2 to 10% hydroxypropyl cellulose, 1 to 5% croscarmellose sodium, and 0.8 to 2% magnesium stearate, is also claimed. Iclepertin is known to be an inhibitor of glycine transporter-1, beta amyloid synthesis and act as a neuroprotectant, useful for treating Alzheimer's disease, Schizophrenia, and cognitive disorder.
advantage: The crystalline forms of compounds are stated to be more stable, and soluble.
biology: No biological data are presented.
examples: An exemplified tablet composition comprising (I), lactose monohydrate, microcrystalline cellulose, hydroxypropyl cellulose and croscarmellose sodium are pre-blended in a suitable freefall blender and further manipulative steps is described (example 8, page 15). Also, the tablets produced therefrom showed good and consistent solubility, DP dissolution after 45 minutes was between 93-97% (example 9, pages 15 and 16).
chemistry: Iclepertin (I) (claim 1, page 16) is specifically claimed for use in the composition.

title: High throughput rna-editing screening methods
enhanced_title: Method of screening a macro-footprint sequence in a candidate guide RNA for editing a target RNA of interest - useful for treating neurological diseases.
novelty: Methods for identifying a guide RNA suitable for editing a target RNA of interest (eg APP, ABCA4, SERPINA1, HEXA or LRRK2) are claimed, comprises contacting a self-annealing RNA or an engineered RNA structure with an RNA editing entity and identifying an edited target RNA, wherein the self-annealing RNA or engineered RNA structure comprises a target RNA, a candidate guide RNA, and a linker that covalently attaches the target and candidate guide RNA, and the RNA editing entity can be an adenosine deaminase acting on RNA (ADAR), an ADAR variant, a catalytically active deaminase domain or a fusion protein comprising all three. A method for producing a vector encoding the guide RNA, the engineered RNA structure and a library of engineered RNA structures are claimed. A method of screening a macro-footprint sequence in a candidate guide RNA having a micro-footprint sequence that configures the candidate guide RNA to facilitate an edit of a base in a target RNA via an RNA editing entity is claimed. It is claimed that the RNA editing entity can be useful as gene therapy for treating neurological diseases, such as Tauopathies, Duchenne's muscular dystrophy, cystic fibrosis, alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency, Parkinson's, Alzheimer's or Stargardt disease.
advantage: No specific advantage is given.
biology: Studies were performed to examine the engineered guide RNAs targeting the G1961E ABCA4 mutation (that causes Stargardt disease) by high throughput screening method. The method identified a superior guide RNA capable of mediating high (> 60%) on-target editing (in contrast to the negligible on-target adenosine editing observed in the A-C mismatch gRNA). Data showed that, yielded designs enabled both ADAR1 and ADAR2 to edit the ABCA4 G1961 target (example 6, page 93, figure 25).
chemistry: Sequences provided in source document.

title: Antibody binding specifically to AIMP2 and/or AIMP2 aggregate
enhanced_title: Antibodies that specifically bind to AIMP2 or its aggregates - useful for diagnosing, treating and screening drugs for treating neurodegenerative diseases.
novelty: An antibody or fragment specific to AIMP2 or its aggregates and having immunological activity is claimed. An isolated nucleic acid molecule encoding the antibody, a vector and a host cell transformed with the vector are claimed. A composition for the prevention or treatment of neurodegenerative diseases comprising the antibody is claimed. A composition for diagnosing neurodegenerative diseases comprising the antibody is claimed. A neurodegenerative disease diagnostic kit comprising the composition is claimed. An information providing method for diagnosing a neurodegenerative disease comprising detecting an AIMP2 monomer or AIMP2 aggregate in a biological sample isolated from a subject using the antibody is claimed. Methods for screening a substance that inhibits the formation of AIMP2 aggregates and treating neurodegenerative diseases (eg glaucoma, stroke, Parkinson's disease, amyotrophic lateral sclerosis, Creutzfeldt Jakob disease and type II diabetes), by reacting the candidate compound with the AIMP2 aggregate, quantifying using the antibody and selecting a compound having reduced amount of AIMP2 aggregates compared to a control group are claimed.
advantage: The antibodies have improved binding affinity and specificity to the AIMP2 aggregates and useful in the diagnosis and treatment of neurodegenerative diseases.
biology: Studies were performed to validate the 5B2 antibody as Parkinson's disease marker using an antibody (pS129-?Syn) that labels ?-synuclein, a Lewy body marker, and 5B2, a target antibody for AIMP2 aggregate, using autopsy temporal lobe brain tissues of Parkinson's disease patients and age-matched normal controls. Results confirmed that ?-synuclein and AIMP2 aggregates were found in the brain tissue of patients with Parkinson's disease at a significant intensity compared to the normal control group. The results of confocal immunofluorescence imaging using the pS129-?-synuclein antibody and the 5B2 antibody on the autopsied temporal lobe brain tissue of a Parkinson's disease patients, confirmed that the signals of ?-synuclein and AIMP2 aggregates were colocalized, indicating that AIMP2 aggregates, can be detected through the 5B2 antibody (example 5, page 41, figure 3).
chemistry: Sequences provided in source document.

(I) was administered at 5mg/kg intraperitoneally and results showed that (I) exerted an antidepressant effect on treatment-resistant depression via the 5HT3 receptor and exerted a therapeutic effect on treatment-resistant depression (test example 2, page 37-38, figure 4-5)

title: Method for detecting mild cognitive impairment and mild Alzheimer's disease
enhanced_title: Method for detecting mild cognitive impairment and/or mild Alzheimer's disease - comprises measuring the level of FGF-19, PLA2G10, CPA2, TIMP4, IGFBP1, NEFL and TMPRSS15.
novelty: A method for detecting mild cognitive impairment and/or mild Alzheimer's disease, comprising measuring the amount or concentration of a biomolecule such as FGF-19, PLA2G10, CPA2 and TIMP4, IGFBP1, NEFL and TMPRSS15 in a biological sample is claimed. A method for detecting mild cognitive impairment and/or mild Alzheimer's disease, comprising measuring the amount or concentration of a biomolecule in a biological sample is claimed, wherein the biomolecule comprises two or more unsaturated bonds. A method for determining a therapeutic effect on mild cognitive impairment and/or mild Alzheimer's disease is claimed. A method for determining the progress of a pathological condition of mild cognitive impairment and/or mild Alzheimer's disease is claimed. The use of proteins biomolecules and triglycerides biomolecules, for the detection or diagnosis of mild cognitive impairment and/or mild Alzheimer's disease is claimed. A method for screening therapeutic agent for treating mild cognitive impairment and/or mild Alzheimer's disease is claimed. A kit for detecting or diagnosing mild cognitive impairment and/or mild Alzheimer's disease is claimed.
advantage: The detection method is easy and accurate.
biology: Experiments were performed to discriminate mild cognitive impairment and mild Alzheimer's disease based on the combination of protein and lipid. Results are tabulated and showed that the combination of 7 kinds of proteins (FGF-19, PLA2G10, CPA2, TIMP4, IGFBP1, NEFL, TMPRSS15) and the lipids was NC and sMCI as compared with the case of a single protein. An increase in AUC was confirmed in the discrimination between NC and MCI. It was confirmed that the combination of these proteins and lipids could discriminate between NC and sMCI and NC and MCI with higher discriminative ability as compared with the case of a single combination (example 5, pages 64-66, figure 30 and 31, tables 13 and 14).
chemistry: It is claimed that treatment for mild cognitive impairment and/or mild Alzheimer's disease is drug therapy, diet and/or exercise therapy.

The compound is disclosed as a prodrug and is useful as an antagonist of mGlu2 and mGlu3 receptor, for treating mood disorder, anxiety disorder, cognitive disorder, Alzheimer's disease, Parkinson's disease, sleep disorder, Huntington chorea, epilepsy, cerebral infarction, and cerebral ischemia.
advantage: The compound has better storage stability and ease of handling.
biology: No biological data are presented.
examples: (I) (466 g) was heated and dissolved with ethyl acetate (1.87 kg) and triethylamine (462 g) at 54 °C, filtered for foreign matter and washed with ethyl acetate (0.48 kg) and heptane (2.39 kg) was added dropwise to the filtrate at 75 °C, stirred and cooled and the suspension was filtered and washed with a mixed solution of ethyl acetate / heptane (1/9, 1.93 kg) and dried to obtain D-type crystals (417 g, white solid) of (I). In a stability study, (I) was stable at 65 °C for 4 weeks and 40°C/75% RH for 3 months (examples 1-2, pages 10-11).

enhanced_title: New benzazepine spiro derivatives are vasopressin V2 receptor antagonists - useful for treating eg hypertension, dysmenorrhea, adrenal hyperplasia, and depression.

The compounds are diclosed to act as vasopressin V2 receptor antagonists.
advantage: No specific advantage is given.
biology: An in vivo test was performed to determine the pharmacokinetics of (I), in mice by intravenous injection and oral administration. The oral administration (10 mg/kg) of (I) showed a T1/2 of 0.6h, Cmax of 1255 ng/mL, AUC0-inf of 4058 ng*h/mL and % bioavailability of 56%; and intravenous administration (1mg/kg) showed a T1/2 of 0.92, AUC0-inf of 719 ng*h/mL and CI of 23.2 mL/mg/kg, which demonstrated that intravenous and oral administration of (I) in mice showed lower metabolic clearance rate Cl in vivo and higher in vivo exposure AUC 0-inf (experimental example 2, pages 48-49; tables 2 and 3).
chemistry: Several compounds including compound 1 (I) (page 40; claim 13, page 54) are specifically claimed. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 51).

advantage: No specific advantage is given.

title: Novel compound modulating GSK-3 activity
enhanced_title: New thiazolidine compounds are GSK-3 down-regulation inducers - useful for treating psychiatric disorders, metabolic disorders, and cancer.
novelty: New thiazolidine compounds, their salts or derivatives, compositions comprising them and process for their preparation are claimed. The compounds are claimed to be used for reating or preventing a GSK-3 mediated disease, such as Alzheimer's disease, psychiatric disorders, metabolic disorders, and cancer. A kit for treating or preventing GSK-3 mediated disease is also claimed. A method for use in regenerative medicine by administering the compositions comprising the compounds is finally claimed. The compounds are disclosed to be used for modulating GSK3 level (e.g., GSK3 (eg GSK3a/GSK3b or GSK3P) or CK1), particularly induce down-regulation of GSK3.
advantage: The compound improves spacial memory, exploratory behavioural, working memory and alleviate behavioural deficit in Alzheimer's mice via downregulating GSK3 and inducing autophagy degradation pathways.
biology: An in vivo test was performed to determine the efficacy of (I) in improving cognition decline and the memory. The performance of the animals in both preventive and therapeutic treatment groups in the Y-maze exploratory behaviour tests was similar to the performance of the wild type animals. The data suggested that (I) has prevented the progression of the disease pathogenesis and memory deficit effectively. While in case of the therapeutic treatment of the (I), it was able to restore the cognitive decline and behavioural deficits. The percent alteration in, both the treatment groups (preventive and therapeutic) were found significantly higher than the vehicle control (page 12, figures 7A-7C).
chemistry: 6-(5-Ethylidene-4-oxo-2thioxo-thiazolidin-3-yl)-hexanoic acid compound with 1H-indole of formula I is (I) (SG001; I) (page 7; claim 1, page 14) is the only compound specifically claimed.

title: Tau biosensor cell lines
enhanced_title: Cell comprising a vector comprising expression cassette comprising polynucleotide encoding tau repeat domain and reporter - useful for detecting seed tau protein in a sample.
novelty: A polynucleotide comprising a polynucleotide encoding tau repeat domain and a polynucleotide encoding a reporter, is claimed. A vector comprising an expression cassette comprising the polynucleotide, is further claimed. A cell comprising the vector, is further claimed. A method of measuring a titer of or detecting attomolar levels of seed tau protein in a sample, or detecting Alzheimer's disease (AD), or neurodegenerative tauopathy disease or condition linked to tau protein aggregation in a subject, is further claimed, by contacting the sample with the cell, performing a seeding assay and detecting tau protein aggregates. A method of identifying a tau protein aggregation inhibitor, is further claimed, by contacting the cell with putative tau protein aggregation inhibitor, performing seeding assay, detecting tau protein aggregates, and identifying tau protein aggregation inhibitor, where the inhibitor interacts with tau protein. A method of identifying tau protein aggregation regulator or modulator, is finally claimed.
advantage: The cell line allows for rapid and reliable detection of tau protein at the attomolar level with improved sensitivity.
biology: Experimental study was conducted to evaluate the ability of v2H biosensor cells to detect mouse brain-derived tau by serially diluting brain extract from a 9-month-old PS19 transgenic mouse expressing full-length human tau containing P301S mutation. The v2H cells were transduced and seeding activity was measured over four log orders of concentration. The lower limit of detection (LLOD) for lysate was ~ 316 pg of total protein. Next frontal cortex homogenates from 5 AD cases were evaluated and the LLOD was 153 pg to 1.2 ng of total protein. FRET positivity resulting from IP followed by seeding assay of spiked samples did not differ between CSF and PBS or with volume of IP. The LLOD in this condition was 31.6 pg of total protein. Further to determine the LLOD of tau seeds in CSF, control CSF were spiked with small quantities of AD frontal cortex protein or recombinant tau fibrils, followed by the seeding assay. Seeding activity was detected in CSF from as little as 31.6 pg of total AD brain protein and 100 attomolar recombinant fibrils (example 2, pages 46 and 47, figures 6 and 7).
chemistry: Sequences provided in source document.

biology: Studies were conducted for evaluating depression in mice models obtained using HM4Gi, which suppresses neural function using adeno-associated virus, where HM4Gi was activated by clozapine-N-oxide (CNO). examples: Methods for production of bipolar disorder and schizophrenia model mouse using lentivirus expressing shRNA capable of selectively suppressing the expression of AMPA receptors (GluA1-4) or scrambled RNA (non-functional shRNA as a control) and analyzing them with Posalt forced swimming test, the tail suspension test, the open field test, the rotarod test, three-chamber social interaction test and the PPI test are described briefly. Also provided are methods for producing autism, depression mouse models (examples 1-6, pages 27-36).

novelty: A spherical culture containing a first cell layer containing nerve stem cells and/or neural progenitor cells and a second cell layer containing brain nerve cells is claimed. A spherical culture produced by the method is claimed. A composition comprising the spherical culture is claimed. Drug screening method that includes a step of contacting the spherical culture with a test substance; measuring the desired properties of the culture supernatant is claimed. Methods for producing spinal motor neurons are claimed, which comprises a step of inoculating pluripotent stem cells into a culture tank equipped with a stirring blade. Methods for producing GABAergic nerve cells or precursor cells thereof are claimed, which comprises seeding pluripotent stem cells in a culture tank equipped with a stirring wing. It is disclosed that the inverted brain organoids could be an in vitro disease model.
advantage: The brain organoid that can be easily quantitatively analyzed in a region containing brain nerve cells and can be suitably used for drug discovery screening of neurological diseases and evaluation systems of compounds.
biology: Experiments were conducted for analysis of Alzheimer's disease using inverted brain organoids. When cultured by vertical mixing for 2 months, the brain organoid showed an inverted structure accompanied by the expression of the hippocampal marker PROX1, which was consistent with the analysis result of scRNA-seq, such that an inverted brain organoid was obtained, and the generation of hippocampal neurons was confirmed. Also, accumulation of A? oligomers was observed in MAP2-positive neurons of AD brain organoids, suggesting that inverted brain organoids could be an in vitro disease model (page 66, figures 17and 19).
examples: Methods for production of a brain organoid using human iPS cells derived from healthy embryoid body formation (201B7 strain) under feeder cell-free conditions are described briefly (examples 1-7, pages 45-48).
chemistry: It is claimed that the brain nerve cell comprises nerve cell selected from GABAergic nerve cells, dopaminergic nerve cells, and hippocampal nerve cells.

examples: Methods for the production of the HpmA EC strain and the preparation of MPTP/p-induced Parkinson's disease early stage mouse model were demonstrated (examples 1 and 11, pages 7 and 12, table 1).

title: A genetically modified immunodeficient mouse expressing human or humanized APP and mutated human PSEN1

A cell from the mouse, is further claimed. A mouse comprising a cell having the same genotype of the cell from the mouse, is further claimed. A progeny mouse of the mouse is further claimed. A method for producing the mouse, is further claimed. A method for producing progeny mouse having characteristics of early-onset AD is further claimed. A method for propagating the progeny mouse, is finally claimed. The mouse is disclosed to be useful for assessing immune interactions with amyloid and assessing an effect of test agent on neuroinflammation, cognitive deficit and to measure short term memory in response to amyloid.
advantage: The mouse models do not develop a large tumor burden, hence can be aged to more relevant time point for studying certain mechanisms and characteristics of AD including cognitive deficit, increased hippocampal plaque deposits or increased neuroinflammation in the brain, relative to a control, while permitting a robust platform for studying immune interactions with amyloid, and exhibit similar amyloid plaque depositions in the hippocampus with minimal cortical deposits, which more closely resembles the human AD pathology.
biology: Behavioral phenotype cognition of the NSG.APP/PSEN1 mouse model was assessed at 7 months on a short-term memory Y-maze task. The results showed that both male and female NSG animals exhibited intact short-term memory, while transgenic NSG.APP/PSEN1 littermates failed this task and spend an equal percentage of time exploring all the arms, thus indicating the utility of NSG.APP/PSEN1 mouse for assessment of immune-related interventions that may prevent cognitive deficits (example 1, page 31, figure 1).

The puromycin resistance cassette is expressed in hiNSC lines regardless of differentiation status, as demonstrated by qPCR analysis. The expression of channelrhodopsin is only observable by qPCR in hiNSC lines that have beeninduced to differentiate, demonstrating the specificity of the synapsin promoter for mature neurons. An increase in mature neuron-specific markers, ie potassium-chloride cotransporter 2 (KCC2) and synaptophysin, in the hiNSC lines (based on qPCR analysis) is correlated with synapsin promoter induced expression of channelrhodopsin (example 2, page 48-49, figure 13-16).

biology: In vitro axonal degeneration assay was performed to test if the newly identified hSARM1 inhibitors can also protect axons from Wallerian degeneration. In this assay, E13.5 mouse DRGs are plated and left to grow axons for 96 hours before severing of the axons proximally to the DRG ganglion. While the severed axons from SARM1-/- mouse remain intact, w.t DRG axons get fragmented and are eliminated almost completely 16 hours post axotomy. Results showed that the most effective compounds in providing high level of protection, while being only very mildly toxic is TK138. TK142 is not toxic at all but provides only mild protection. Similarly, TK174 did not protect axons after axotomy and was not toxic. It is possible, that the reason why TK174 and TK142 are neither toxic nor protective, is due to impermeability of these compounds into the neurons' cytosol (example 8, page 58, figure 15A-15C).

Coefficients of variation were around 10% per sample type. UBE3A expression was robustly reduced in all three brain regions in AS rats. Similar to mice, UBE3A levels in the cerebellum of control rats were lower compared to hippocampus and cortex, while residual UBE3A levels in the AS rat cerebellum were higher (27% of control levels in cerebellum vs. ?10% of control levels in hippocampus and cortex). Also claimed is a process for the purification of carboprost comprising the steps of macrolactonization of carboprost containing 1 to 10% 5,6-trans isomer and 1 to 50% 15(R)-epimer, to form a compound 3b, followed by chromatographic separation of isomers and hydrolysis. Further claimed is a process for preparing an intermediate of carboprost. Novel intermediate of carboprost, is also claimed. Novel crystalline forms of carboprost tromethamine, processes for their preparation and compositions comprising them are finally claimed. Carboprost is known to be a PGF2 alpha agonist and act as abortifacient, useful for treating birth complication and planned abortion.
advantage: The process is simple, efficient, cost-effective, reduces the formation of impurities or isomers and provides compounds with higher yield and purity with good stability as compared to prior art processes.
biology: No biological data are presented.
examples: The synthesis and purification of (II) via intermediate (I) is described; Compound of (II) (10.0 g) was dissolved in 1 L acetonitrile containing water of 0.02% at 85 °C to give a homogeneous solution and then cooled down the homogeneous solution at 60 °C, and the white solid was slowly started to precipitate. The mixture was further cooled down to RT. The resulting precipitate crystal was filtered and dried to yield crystalline form of (I) (9.0 g) (examples 1-11, pages 18-24). Also, (II) was found to be more stable at 20 °C for 6 months, 5 °C for 18 months, and 80 °C for 3 days (example 12, pages 24 and 25).

title: Retinal organoid model systems
enhanced_title: Retinal organoid model system comprising hPSC-derived photoreceptor cells - useful as a model for testing a therapy against genetic diseases, including thalassemia.
novelty: Retinal organoid model system that comprises a population of human pluripotent stem cell (hPSC)-derived photoreceptor (PR) cells are claimed, wherein the PR cells are adapted to elaborate an interphotoreceptor matrix (IPM) with visible outer segments on its surface upon restoration of function of a gene encoding a structural component of the IPM, and wherein the PR cells comprise a recombinant gene encoding a structural component of the IPM. A method of testing for the efficacy of a therapeutic treatment using the retinal organoid model system by administering a candidate therapeutic treatment for restoring function of the gene encoding the structural component of the IPM, visualizing the photoreceptor outer segments within the IPM and detecting a change in the IPM is claimed. A method of testing for an effective therapeutic treatment for a genetic mutation using the retinal organoid model system is claimed, wherein the genetic diseases can be cystic fibrosis, sickle-cell anemia, hemochromatosis, Huntington's disease, Duchenne's muscular dystrophy, Tay-Sachs disease, Angelman syndrome, ankylosing spondylitis, Marfan syndrome, or thalassemia. Methods of making the retinal organoid model system are claimed.
advantage: The screening method is easier, faster sensitive, robust and reproducible.
biology: Studies were performed to assess the sensitivity of the model system to restore outer segments on the surface of ROs explored using mixed cultures of wild type and IMPG2 mutant hPSC lines by light microscopic imaging. Results showed 100% WT control hPSC-RO with thick photoreceptor outer segments, which demonstrated a 50:50 WT:MT hPSC-RO culture that displayed a moderate amount of photoreceptor outer segments, although reduced compared to 100% WT ROs. A further decrease in the presence of photoreceptor outer segments was observed with ratios of 20:80 and 5:95 (example 4, pages 22 and 23, figures 4 and 5).
examples: Methods for the construction of hPSC retinal organoid were described (example 1, pages 18 and 19).
chemistry: Sequences provided in source document.

(I) showed agonistic activity (EC50) values (in ?M) of 0.102, 0.378, >10, 0.294, 8.10 and 0.0364 against HTR2A, HTR2B, HTR1A, HTR1B, HTR6 and DRD2S; antagonistic activity (IC50) values ((in ?M) 0.302, 0.0637, 1.74 and 0.607 against HTR2A, HTR2B, ADRA1A and HRH1 and blocking activity at a concentration of 1.73, 0.960 and 5.45 against SERT, DAT and NET, respectively (example19, pages 129-135; table 28).
