title: Compositions of induced pluripotent stem cell-derived cells and methods of use thereof
enhanced_title: Cell culture comprising induced pluripotent stem cell-derived microglia, astrocytes, and/or neurons in media - useful for screening therapeutic compound for treating neurodegenerative disease.
novelty: A cell culture comprising induced pluripotent stem cell (iPSC)-derived microglia, astrocytes, and/or neurons in media is claimed. A method for screening a therapeutic compound for treating a neurodegenerative disease comprising contacting a test compound with a culture and measuring the functional activity of the cells, is claimed. It is also claimed that a decrease in complement C3 released from the microglia indicates that the therapeutic compound is capable of treating a neurodegenerative disease. The use of the culture as a model of a neurodegenerative disease is claimed. A method of screening for a neurodegenerative disease comprising detecting a level of soluble TREM2 in a culture using ELISA, is also claimed, wherein the method further comprising detecting the level of COMT, NRXN2 and/or SST in microglia. It is also claimed that the neurodegenerative disease is Alzheimer's disease or multiple sclerosis.
advantage: The cell culture provides very accurate model to understand complex interactions between human microglia, neurons, astrocytes in a 2D or 3D organoid systems and mimic neurogenerative diseases.
biology: Assay was performed for the characterization of role of TREM2 in microglia. To better understand the role of TREM2 mutations on AD risk, heterozygous TREM2 loss of function microglia were generated. Validation of partial loss of TREM2 microglia identified pathways unique to HetTREM2 KO supporting the notion that heterozygous TREM2 KO microglia better model the phenotype that arises from the inheritance of TREM2 mutations that lead to increased AD risk. Gene expression of the master transcriptional regulator of cholesterol biosynthesis, SREBF2 was significantly reduced resulting in concomitant reduction of cholesterol biosynthetic enzyme genes, suggesting down-regulation of cholesterol synthesis due to partial but not complete loss of TREM2 function (example 13, page 101-107, figure 67-68).
chemistry: Sequences provided in source document.

novelty: New ganglio-oligosaccharides, compositions comprising them and their use for treating and diagnosing neurodegenerative disease, eg Alzheimer's, Parkinson's and Huntington's disease (HD), frontotemporal dementia, Friedreich's ataxia, age-related macular degeneration, and Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease, are claimed. Also claimed is a kit, comprising the compounds and a second component. A method for making a prognosis of a neurodegenerative disease via use of a biological sample by mixing it with compounds to form a first immunocomplex; reacting it with an anti-IgM antibody, is further claimed. The compound are disclosed to be biomarkers.
advantage: No specific advantage is given.
biology: The use of the compounds in evaluating the occurrence or the likelihood of occurrence of HD was examined. Only auto-IgM, but not auto-IgG, antibodies showed differences between the controls and the diseased samples, probably due to low immunogenicity of glycan in human body. Specifically, the anti-glycan IgM levels were elevated in pre-HD cases. Combining age and repeat number improved the AUC to 0.86 (95% CI, 0.74 - 0.99). When (I) was further combined, the AUC score could reach 0.95 (95% CI, 0.85-1). The combination indicated the best discrimination capacity to differentiate NC and pre-HD group (example 1, pages 56-66; figure 1; tables 1-3)
chemistry: Five compounds are specifically claimed, eg 5-aminopentyl-4-O-(4-O-(2-acetoamino-3-O-(?-D-galactopyranoside)-2-deoxy-?-D-galactopyranoside)-3-O-(5-acetoamino-3,5-dideoxy-8-O-(5-acetoamino-3,5-dideoxy-D-glycero-?-D-galacto-2-nonulopyranosylonate)-D-glycero-?-D-galacto-2-nonulopyranoylonate)-?-D-galactopyranoside)-?-D-glucopyranoside (compound G20; compound 5; compound 1-5; GD1b; I) (pages 12 and 46; claim 2, page 74). A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 73).

biology: An in vivo experiment was carried out to estimate the efficacy of (I) (10 mg/kg) on spatial working memory in mice subjected to CRS. (I) (30 mg/kg) showed significant reduction of alteration rate (p< 0.001) caused by chronic restraint stress, reversing working memory deficits and impairments (p<0.05) in the mice (page 46; fig 1). Further in an in vitro assay, (I) exhibited an EC50 values (in ?M) of 10.67, 1.91, 3.52, < 0.03 against human ?1?3?2, a2?3?2, a3?3?2, and a5?3?2 GABAA receptors transfected HEK293T cells, respectively (pages 53 and 54).

title: Novel compound and use thereof for treating emotional behavioral disorders
enhanced_title: New substituted piperazine-1-carboxamide derivatives are agmatinase inhibitors - useful for treating emotional and behavioral, disorders.
novelty: New substituted piperazine-1-carboxamide derivatives, their salts, stereoisomers, compositions comprising them, and their use for treating emotional and behavioral, disorders, their symptoms (autism, schizophrenia, obsessive-compulsive disorder, depression, anxiety disorder, panic disorder, attention deficit hyperactivity disorder), and neurodegenerative diseases, are claimed. The compounds are disclosed to act as agmatinase inhibitors.
advantage: The compounds have excellent efficacy.
biology: An in vivo experiment was carried out to estimate the sociability and cognitive improvement effects of the test compounds against valproic acid-induced autism in mice. (I) showed significant reduction on sniffing time in a chamber and retention time over control (pages 37 and 38; figs 2 and 3).
chemistry: Several compounds including N-(3-chlorobenzoyl)piperazine-1-carboxamide 2,2,2-trifluoroacetate (I) (compound 37, example 9, pages 4, 5, 10, and 24; claim 5, page 42) are specifically claimed. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 39).

title: N-Sulfonylurea derivatives and the therapeutic use thereof
enhanced_title: New N-sulfonylurea derivatives are NLRP3 inflammasome inhibitors - useful for treating rheumatoid arthritis, Alzheimer's disease, atherosclerosis and diabetes.
novelty: New N-sulfonylurea derivatives, compositions and combinations comprising them, a process for their preparation, are claimed. The compositions are claimed to be useful for treating inflammatory diseases associated with NLRP3 flammability including rheumatoid arthritis, Crohn's disease, Alzheimer's disease, Parkinson's disease, stroke, atherosclerosis, diabetes, myocardial infarction, psoriasis and metabolic diseases. The compounds are claimed to act as NLRP3 inflammasome inhibitors.
advantage: No specific advantage is given.
biology: In an in vivo experiment was carried out to estimate the efficacy of (I) on uric acid crystal induced mechanical allodynia in C57BL/6 mice. (I) (10 mg/kg) showed up to 81% reduction on the mechanical allodynia in the mice (pages 26-28; fig 2C).
chemistry: Several compounds including 1-(3,5-dimethylphenyl)-3-((4-(2-hydroxypropan-2-yl)furan-2-yl)sulfonyl)urea (I) (compound 4b, page 17; claim 6, page 30) are specifically claimed. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 29).

title: Means and method for the diagnosis and treatment of autism spectrum disorders based on the detection and modulation of a deubiquitinase
enhanced_title: Method for diagnosing autism spectrum disorder or intellectual disability - comprising conducting assay to identify expression of CYLD gene/protein in biological sample.
novelty: A method for diagnosing the presence or an increased risk of developing an autism spectrum disorder, or intellectual Disability (ID), is claimed, comprising conducting an assay to identify whether there is a variant sequence or multiple variant sequences, in the subject's nucleic acid, wherein the variant sequences is in portion of CYLD. A method for identifying mutations correlated with the presence or increased risk of developing an autism spectrum disorder, or ID is also claimed. A method for screening compounds or compositions for a modulator of a neuropsychiatric manifestation associated with an autism spectrum disorder, or ID, comprising contacting candidate compound or composition with a CYLD protein and/or CYLD nucleic acid, and determining activity and/or stability of the CYLD protein in presence and in absence of the candidate compound or composition, is claimed. An in-vivo method for screening compounds or compositions for a modulator of a neuropsychiatric manifestation associated with an autism spectrum disorder, or ID is also claimed. The use of compound or composition in the treatment of an autism spectrum disorder, or ID, in a subject, is also claimed. A genetically modified non-human animal, preferably a mouse or rat is finally claimed.
advantage: The method has improved accuracy.
biology: Assay was performed to cross Cyld-/- and Shank3-/- mice and analyze the neurobehavioral phenotype of the resulting Cyld-/-Shank3-/- double mutant mice, in order to clarify if CYLD deletion can modify the autistic phenotype of Shank3-/- mice. A significant difference in the latency to reach the platform was detectable at day 2 between control and Cyld-/- mice, and at day 3 between control and Shank3-/- mice. At day 3 of reversal learning, the inventors could also detect a significant difference in the distance travelled between Cyld-/-, Shank3-/-, Cyld-/-Shank3-/- and controls, respectively. To sum up, Cyld-/- and Shank3-/- mutants show similar impairments in the social and cognitive domains, but opposite phenotypes in the repetitive domain. Neither phenotype is restored or exacerbated in Cyld-/-Shank3-/- double mutants whose repetitive behavioral profile rather resembles the one of single Shank3-/-mutants (example 2, page 24-25, figure 1-2).
chemistry: Sequences provided in source document.

title: Alzheimer's disease biomarker L-valine and application thereof
enhanced_title: Application of Alzheimer's disease biomarker - for diagnosing Alzheimer's disease and in screening drugs for treating and/or preventing Alzheimer's disease.
novelty: A biomarker for diagnosing Alzheimer's disease (AD) comprises L-valine is claimed. The application of the AD biomarker in constructing an early diagnosis model of AD and/or preparing an early diagnosis device for AD is claimed. An early diagnosis model of AD is claimed. An early diagnosis device for AD is claimed. The application of the AD biomarker in screening drugs for treating and/or preventing AD is claimed.
advantage: The application of detecting the level of L-valine in feces can assist in the early diagnosis of AD, contributes to non-invasive rapid detection, and has the characteristics of timeliness, convenience, high specificity, and high sensitivity.
biology: The qualitative and quantitative analysis of metabolites was carried out on fecal samples of 9-month-old AD model mice (APP/PS1 transgenic mice) and wild-type (WT) mice. Results showed that the level of L-valine in the feces samples of AD model mice was significantly higher than that of wild-type mice, indicating that L-valine in the feces can be used as AD ie a biomarker for AD, which can assist in the early diagnosis of Alzheimer's disease by detecting the level of L-valine in feces (example 1, pages 7-9, figure 1, table 1).
chemistry: It is also claimed that the level of biomarker can be detected using a tandem time-of-flight mass spectrometer.

title: Taurine as biomarker for alzheimer's disease and use thereof
enhanced_title: Alzheimer's disease biomarker such as taurine - useful for diagnosing and screening Alzheimer's disease.
novelty: An Alzheimer's disease biomarker taurine is claimed. Also claimed use of Alzheimer's disease biomarkers in Alzheimer's disease early diagnosis model and/or preparing an Alzheimer's disease early diagnosis device. Also claimed an Alzheimer's disease early diagnosis model wherein the input variables of the Alzheimer's disease early diagnosis model include the peak intensity value of the taurine mass spectrum. Also claimed early diagnosis model of Alzheimer's disease according to claim 4, wherein the criterion for judging the positive Alzheimer's disease is the differential expression multiple ? 8.51. Also cliamed an early diagnosis device for Alzheimer's disease. Also claimed use of the Alzheimer's disease biomarker in the screening of drugs for the treatment and / or prevention of Alzheimer's disease.
advantage: The method offers non-invasive rapid detection, and has the characteristics of timeliness, convenience, high specificity and high sensitivity.
biology: An in vivo study was conducted to detect the level of (I) in cerebral cortex samples of 9-month-old AD model mice and wild-type (WT) mice. Results showed for the first time that the level of (I) in fecal samples of Alzheimer's disease is significantly higher than that in normal stool samples, VIP levels with mannose, inositol and glycine were found to be 2.67, 10.80 and 1.93 respectively (example 2, page 9-11; table 2, page 11).
chemistry: Taurine is (I) (claim 1, page 12).

title: Alzheimer's disease biomarker, and screening method therefor and application thereof
enhanced_title: Alzheimer's disease biomarker such as 11(Z),14(Z)-eicosadienoic acid - useful for diagnosing and screening Alzheimer's disease.
novelty: An Alzheimer's disease biomarker with 11(Z),14(Z)-eicosadienoic acid is claimed. Also claimed an early diagnostic model of Alzheimer's disease whose input variables include the mass spectral peak intensity values of the Alzheimer's disease biomarker 1(Z),11(Z)-eicosadienoic acid. Also claimed a screening method for Alzheimer's disease biomarkers comprising collecting a sample, detecting the sample, and structural identification and data analysis of the metabolite in the sample. Also claimed a multidimensional statistical analysis comprises constructing an orthogonal partial least squares-discriminant analysis model, screening the metabolite of VIP>1 in the detection results as an Alzheimer's disease biomarker. Also claimed use of the Alzheimer's disease biomarker in the preparation of an Alzheimer's disease diagnostic tool, comprising a device for predicting the risk of Alzheimer's disease and / or an Alzheimer's disease diagnostic kit. Also claimed a device for predicting the risk of Alzheimer's disease comprising detection unit for qualitative quantitative analysis of the Alzheimer's disease biomarker, control unit for secondary verification, confirmation and quality evaluation of experimental data on the results of qualitative and quantitative analysis of the Alzheimer's disease biomarker; and the evaluation unit, which takes the quantitative results of the Alzheimer's disease biomarker as an input variable, and gives a risk score after comprehensive calculation, thereby predicting the risk of developing Alzheimer's disease; Wherein the detection unit uses liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry to qualitatively and quantitatively analyze the Alzheimer's disease biomarker. Also claimed use of an Alzheimer's disease biomarker in the preparation of drugs for the treatment or diagnosis of Alzheimer's disease.
advantage: The method is convenient, rapid, safe and non-invasive.
biology: An in vivo study was conducted to detect (I) in the hippocampus of 9-month-old AD model mice. Results showed that that there are significant differences in the peak intensity of (I) mass spectrometry in the brain hippocampus of AD model mice in the experimental group and wild-type control mice. The VIP of (I) was 1.08 and the p-value was 0.031, and results showed that the content of (I) in the brain hippocampus of AD model mice was significantly higher than that in wild-type control mice (example 2, page 7-8; table 2, page 8; figure 2, page 5).
chemistry: 11(Z),14(Z)-Eicosadienoic acid is (I) (claim 1, page 10).

The compounds are claimed to be useful in the preparation of G protein-coupled receptor agonist or partial agonist, such as dopamine receptor (dopamine receptor D2) and/or 5-serotonin receptor (5-HT receptor 5-HT 2A and/or 5-HT receptor 5-HT 1A). The compounds are claimed to be useful for treating diseases related to G protein-coupled receptors, such as neurodegenerative diseases, mental disorders and metabolic diseases, such as Parkinson's disease, Alzheimer's disease, bipolar disorder, depression, ADHD, restless legs syndrome, Huntington's disease, male erectile dysfunction, prolactinoma or drug addiction.
advantage: The compounds have good affinity and/or agonistic activity on dopamine receptors and serotonin receptors.
biology: An in vivo test was performed to determine the effects of test compounds on their depression-like behaviors were detected by tail suspension and forced swimming experiments using C57B6 mice which were induced to develop depression-related behavioral representations. The test results of "depression-like" behavior showed that an isomer of (I) could significantly inhibit the restraint-induced "depression-like" behavior at 0.025mg/kg 0.1mg/kg and with immobility duration of 87.04 and 54.13, respectively (test example 6, table 8; page 45).
chemistry: Several substituted thiophene ring compounds are specifically claimed, eg 1,1-dimethyl-3-(4-(2-(6a,7,9,10-tetrahydropyrazino[1,2-a]thieno[4,3,2-de]quinolin-8(6H)-yl)ethyl)trans-cyclohexyl)urea (I-27) (I) (example 27, page 32; claim 7, page 54). An isomer of (I) is disclosed as (-)-I-27 (page 33). A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 46).

biology: An in vivo study was performed to determine the effect of (I) in six male golden hamsters. Results, the average drug concentration value after 8h in group 1 (the dosage of 1.2mg) was 13513ng/ml, and in group 2 (the dosage of 4mg) was 24223 ng/ml (example 4, pages 18 and 19; tables 6 ad 7).
examples: Compound of (I) was stirred with 1,5-naphthalene disulfonic acid (acid/base molar ratio is 1:1) in EtOH, separate solid and vacuum dry at RT to yield crystalline form of (I) (example 2, page 16). Also, (I) was found to be more stable at 60 °C for 0-30 days (experimental example 1, pages 25 and 26).

title: Modulation of microRNA-335-5p for the treatment of sodium channelopathies

title: Machine learing based method of screening potential drug candidate, and a method thereof

The method involves measuring electroencephalogram (EEG) or electromyogram (EMG) data of an animal species eg rodent or human, during administration of a seizure-inducing agent. A machine learning (ML) model is trained with the animal species EEG or EMG data. A potential drug candidate is screened by administering the drug candidate to the species and measuring the data of the species. The measured data taken during the candidate administration is applied to the ML model to determine whether a neurological adverse event is associated with the candidate. The ML model is trained to evaluate change in power of frequency, a polyspike sharp wave or an amplitude of EGG or EMG.
advantage: The method enables screening a potential drug candidate to treat a disease and/or condition based on assessment of EEG or EMG with preliminary safety evaluation effect in a reliable, high accuracy, efficient way and reproducible manner.

advantage: The method greatly improves the probability of finding effective AD drug candidates and vastly reduces developmental costs and accelerating precision drug identification.

examples: To (I) (20 mg) was added a minimal amount of MeOH, resulting in a clear solution. Diethyl ether (5 mL) was added at ambient temperature and allowed to precipitate, and the mother liquor was decanted and isolated the solid to obtain form A of (I) (page 367). The compounds are stated to act as immuno modulators by decreasing decreasing neutrophils, reduces urine protein levels in the mammal, increases splenic CD25+ FOXP3+ regulatory T cells in the mammal, and/or decreases splenic CD4+ IL17+ cells and decreases splenic CD4+ TNF+ T cells.

biology: An in vivo test was performed to determine the efficacy of (I) using C57BL/6 mice infected with influenza A (H1N1). Graphical results showed that (I) at a dose of of 25 mg/kg orally via gavage delayed first mortality by two days and resulted in 83% survival at day 10, whereas vehicle treatment resulted in mortality beginning at day 6 and reaching 25% survival by day 10 (example 5, page 10; figure 4).

Aprocitentan is disclosed to be an endothelin receptor antagonist, preferably known to be ET-A and ET-B antagonists and is disclosed to be useful for treating endothelin related diseases such as hypertension, cerebral ischemia, dementia, myocardial ischemia and other diseases such as diabetes, glaucoma, inflammation, prostate cancer, gram negative septicemia or shock.
advantage: The process is simple and reduces undesired side products and is safe without lowering efficiency of the manufacturing process.
biology: No biological data are presented.
examples: The synthesis of (II) crude form via intermediate (I) is described. The aqueous layer of the reaction mixture was seeded with form A crystals of (I) (6.2 g) in EtOAc and stirred at 25°C for 2h and then, cooled down to -8 °C for 6h, filtered off and washed with IPA (1 kg) and IPA/H2O (90:10 wt/wt) (1 kg) and the solid was dried to obtain form A of (I) (example 2, pages 27-28).

Potassium salt of (5-(4-bromophenyl)-6-(2-((5-bromopyrimidin-2-yl)oxy)ethoxy)pyrimidin-4-yl)(sulfamoyl)amide is (II) (also known as aprocitentan potassium or ACT-132577 potassium, formula Ia, page 1; claim 1, page 30).

Psilocybin (0.1-10 mg/kg SC) produced a dose related increase in the incidence of head twitch response, which reached a peak at the 1 mg/kg dose. 5-MeO DMT (1-30 mg/kg SC) produced a monotonic increase in head twitches. In contrast with psilocybin and 5-MeO DMT, (I) (0.3-30 mg/kg SC) elicited only a modest head twitch response with a peak response of 4.2 ± 0.7 twitches at 30 mg/kg although the lower doses were also associated with incidence of head twitches. No overt signs of hypolocomotion or 5-HT syndrome was recorded over this dose range, except for 2/5 (40%) mice pretreated with 30 mg/kg dose, where some 5-HT syndrome associated behaviours were noted. Therefore the low expression of head twitches, at least over the 0.3-10 mg/kg dose range, did not appear to be a consequence of alternative, competing behaviours (example B3, pages 131-133; figure 1; table 6).

The compounds are dsiclosed to modulate circadian rhythms, ie increases the period of the circadian cycle.
advantage: Compounds has major advantages over the use of hypnotics, which has disruption of circadian rhythms; and over melatonin, and act on circadian oscillator in cells/tissues outside SCN.
biology: In an in vitro test, male and female mPer2Luc mice were maintained in a light / dark cycle with 12 hours of light and 12 hours of darkness (LD12: 12), were killed, and their brains were removed and 250 pm thick SCN slices in ice-cold HBSS medium were prepared using a vibratome. Two explants containing suprachiasmatic nuclei (SCN) from each brain were prepared and treated with the test compounds. (I) increased the circadian rhythm period in the complex tissue of the central pacemaker and can be used to manipulate the phase of the circadian rhythm, for example to synchronize the internal circadian clock with solar time. The overall effect on the circadian oscillator indirectly, but unequivocally, demonstrates that the compound can also be used to modulate the concentration and activity of other components of the oscillator, including the critical components Bmal, Per2, Cry and Clock over time (example 14, pages 14, 33 and 34; figures 4 and 5).
chemistry: Several compounds are exemplified, eg 2,6-dichloro-9-(tetrahydrofuran-2-yl)-9H-purine (I) (compound 4, table 2, page 19; example 1, page 21). No compounds are specifically claimed. A markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 36).

biology: No biological data are presented.
examples: Free acid of (I) (50 mg), methanol (5 mL) and 1 M sodium hydroxide (1 eq) were mixed and evaporated. The solid residue was collected, washed with acetonitrile (0.2 mL), and dried for 1 h at reduced pressure of 2 mbar to obtain Form 1 of (I) (example 1a, page 30). In a stability study, Form 1 of (I) was found to be stable at 40 °C and 60 °C for 7 days and at 80 °C for 3 days with 11 %RH and 75 %RH compared to Form A of (I) (page 32; table 5).

title: Amorphous (a-polymorphic) psilocybin

The solution was transferred to round bottom flask and filtered via 0.2 m PTFE syringe filter and lyophilized at room temperature for 24 hours to obtain amorphous form of (I) (55 mg). The lyophilization yielded a fine colorless powder (example 3, pages 49-53).

chemistry: Several heterocyclic compounds are specifically claimed, including compound I-1 (I) (page 114, table 1; claim 45, page 421). A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 399).

The compounds are claimed to be useful for treating, regulating and/or preventing diseases mediated by miR-124, eg Alzheimer's disease, Parkinson's disease, brain, gastric, liver, lung, intestinal, breast cancers, new coronavirus pneumonia, AIDS and rheumatoid arthritis. The compounds are stated to act as miR-124 inducers.
advantage: The compounds have high activity, metabolic stability and/or high safety.
biology: An in vivo test was performed to determine the effect of (I) using DSS-induced inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) in mice. Results showed that the animals treated withe (I) (40 mg/kg) showed 2.12% increase in body weight compared to a 5.37% decrease with ABX464. (I) also significantly reduced the Disease Activity Index (DAI) score to 8.57, representing a 40.90% reduction from the model group's score of 14.5, while ABX464 only reduced the DAI by 5.72%. Further (I) also restored colon length to 100% of the normal control level, demonstrating superior efficacy in mitigating colon shortening compared to other compounds and ABX464 (example 252, pages 80-83; tables 2-4).

biology: In an in vitro assay, the IC50 values (in nM) of (I) and ABX-1431 against MAGL and FAAH were found to be 1-10 and > 10000; and 1-10 and > 50000 against MAGL and FAAH, respectively (example 2, pages 289-294; ).

novelty: New heterocyclic derivatives, their salts, hydrates and solvates, nanoparticle composition comprising them, process for their preparation and their use as mitochondrial targeting agents for treating mitochondrial dysfunction, eg cancer, ischemic cerebral disease, immune disease, anemia, Parkinson's disease, amyotrophic lateral sclerosis, diabetes, obesity, and depression, are claimed. The compounds are further claimed to increase the expression of Th1 (CD4+, IFN-r+), Th2 (CD4+, IL-4+), and Th17 (CD4+, IL-17+) cells.
advantage: The manufacturing method has the advantages of having fewer steps, taking less time, and having a significantly high yield. Also, the compounds targets mitochondria.
biology: In an in vivo test, 8-week-old SKG mice were immunized by subcutaneous injection (SC) of vimentin protein into the nape of the neck at weeks 0 and 2. Then, 100 ?L of bleomycin (BLM) at a concentration of 0.5 mg/mL was subcutaneously injected for 21 days and then gold nanoparticles containing (I) were subcutaneously injected twice a week at a concentration of 0.5 mg/kg. Seven weeks after induction of systemic sclerosis, mice from each group were humanely sacrificed, and the serum of the sacrificed mice was obtained and vimentin antigen-specific IgG and IgG1 were confirmed through ELISA. Compared to the SSc-V group, it was confirmed that IgG and IgG1 were decreased in the (I) group. Therefore, it was confirmed that gold nanoparticles containing (I) reduced vimentin-specific autoantigens in vivo (example 9, page 19; figures 14 and 15).
chemistry: ((6-((3-(3-Carbamimidoyl-1-methylguanidino)propyl)amino)-6-oxohexyl)triphenylphosphonium (compound JH-001; compound of chemical formula 1;I) (example 1, page 16 and 17; claim 1, page 21) is the only specifically claimed.

biology: In an in vivo experiment, (I) (15 mg/kg) showed significant reduction on random blood glucose level, fasting blood glucose level and body weight and enhances the glucose tolerance over PF06882961 (15/kg) in hGLP1R transgenic mouse type II diabetes model induced by high-fat diet and streptozotocin (example 52, pages 106 and 107; figs 3-5). In another in vivo experiment, the percentage oral bioavailability of (I) and PF06882961 were found to be 34.9 and 13.5% in SD rats, respectively (example 54, pages 108 and 109; table 11).

novelty: New substituted aromatic heterocyclic cyclohexyl aminoalkyl piperidine derivatives, their salts, stereoisomers or tautomers, compositions comprising them, process for their preparation and their use as activators of 5-HT1A receptor, dopamine D2 and D3 receptors for treating central nervous system diseases eg Parkinson's disease, schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, depression, Huntington's disease, Alzheimer's disease, vascular dementia, neuropathic pain, glaucoma and retinal edema, are claimed.
advantage: The compounds are selective D3 agonists, and effectively reduce various toxic side effect.
biology: An in vivo test was performed to evaluate the effect of the compounds on Tacrine-induced mandibular tremor behavior using male Sprague Dawley rats. Graphically depicted results showed that (I) (injected intraperitoneally at a dose of 10 mg/kg) significantly inhibited Tacrine-induced mandibular tremor (mandibular vibration frequency was 0 in 5 min) when compared to control (mandibular vibration frequency was >300 in 5 min) (test example 6, pages 61 and 62; figure 2).
chemistry: Several compounds including (S)-4-(((2-amino-4,5,6,7-tetrahydrobenzo[d]thiazol-6-yl)(propyl)amino)methyl)-N,N-dimethylpiperidine-1-carboxamide (I) are specifically claimed (example 32, page 47; claim 8, page 68). A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 63).

title: Crystal form of heterocyclic compound, preparation method therefor, and use thereof

novelty: New crystalline polymorphic form (designated as Form B) of a substituted fused triazolo derivative and its solvates (designated as Forms A, C to I, K to P, R, S and T), and processes for their preparation and compositions comprising them are claimed. Also claimed is their use as a TRPC5 inhibitor, for treating TRPC5-mediated diseases such as Parkinson's disease, lupus nephritis, brain tumor, renal diseases, stroke, Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease, amyotrophic lateral sclerosis, epilepsy, anxiety or psychiatric disorders. The free form of the derivative is known from WO2022001767.
advantage: The forms have good chemical and physical stability, good drug prospects and preparation processability.
biology: An in vivo study was conducted to determine the pharmacokinetic properties of (I) in SD rats by single intragastric administration. The mean Cmax (ng/mL), Tmax (h), AUClast (ng/mL*hr), AUCINF (ng/mL* hr) of 9537, 1.67, 89167 and 107237, respectively, which are better compared to compound 1-1 that showed respective values of 1217, 4, 14307 and 17832 (pages 42-48; tables 20-27).
chemistry: Compound of formula I (I) (page 2; claim 1, page 49) and its several solvates are specifically claimed. Crystalline Form A of solvate of (I) (400 mg) was added to water (4 mL) and stirred at 50 °C for 30 min to form a suspension. The seed crystal of Form B of (I) (5 mg) was added, placed into a temperature-raising and cooling cycle machine, and stirred the suspension at 50 °C to 5 °C for 6 cycles at a temperature-raising and cooling rate of 0.1°C/min. Centrifuged and filtered the solid and vacuum dried at 50 °C for 2 h to obtain Form B of (I) (example 2, pages 29-30; table 2). In a stability study, Form B of (I) was found to be stable when placed under 60 °C closed conditions, 40°C/75%RH exposure conditions, and 25°C/92%RH exposure condition for one week (page 40; table 18; figures 72-79).

Also claimed processes for preparing the pyridazine compound. Novel intermediates of the pyridazine compound and process for their preparation, are also claimed. The compound is first disclosed in WO2022166890 and disclosed to be inhibit the activity of NLRP3 inflammasome, activation of downstream caspase-1, thereby inhibiting the maturation and secretion of IL-1?.
advantage: The crystalline forms of compounds are stated to be more stable, soluble, and hygroscopic nature and the process is simple, efficient, cost-effective, easy to operate, less reaction steps, commercially available and suitable for large scale industrial production, and avoids the use of purification and provides compounds with higher yield and purity.
biology: An in vivo study, (I) significantly improved the effect on the spatial learning and memory impairment caused by oA? in mice, wherein the behavior score in mouse of 63.08 (example 5, pages 31-33; table 26).
chemistry: Compound AA is (I) (claim 26, page 37). Free base of a specific pyridazine compound and its salt, including compound of formula II is (II) (claim 11, page 34) are specifically claimed. The synthesis of (II) via intermediate (I) is described (examples 1-3, pages 19-22). The amorphous form of (II) (1.0 g) was dissolved in acetone and stirred, followed by addition of D-malic acid acetone solution, and then again treated with acetone. The reaction solution was suspended and stirred at 50 °C for 24 h, cooled to RT and filtered, and vacuum dried to obtain the crystalline Form B of (I) (example 5, page 23). Also, (II) was found to be more stable at 60 °C, 25 °C/92.5% RH, 40 °C/75% RH, 60 °C/75% RH and 30°C/65% RH for 5 and 10 days, respectivelty (example 7, pages 23 and 24).

title: Inhibitors and uses therefor

novelty: The use of new and known substituted benzopyrrole derivatives, their salts, compositions comprising them, and their use as NSP5 inhibitors for treating or inhibiting the development of a coronavirus infection, which is capable of causing severe acute respiratory syndrome (SARS); neurological symptoms, eg headache, fatigue, dizziness, difficulty sleeping, anxiety, depression and impaired or loss of smell (anosmia); and acute inflammatory condition, are claimed.
advantage: The compounds selectively reduces neuronal cell death caused by NSP5.
biology: In an in vivo test, 5-month-old female C57BL/6 wildtype mice were injected intravenously with PHP.B hSyn-NSP5-V5 adeno-associated virus (AAV) and treated daily with (I) (10 mg/kg), Paxlovid (nirmatrelvir/ritonavir) (10 mg/kg nirmatrelvir: 3.33 mg/kg ritonavir) or a vehicle control. Motor testing was performed on the Rota-rod five times daily over a three-day testing period. Average weight loss following AAV injection was comparable for all treatment groups until 17 days post injection, at which point weight loss was less severe in (I)-treated mice than both Paxlovid- and vehicle-treated controls, reaching significance at day 19 and continuing until end of testing. Also, motor coordination testing in the hanging wire test at both early and later stage resulted in more (I)-treated mice reaching the maximum time hanging upside down on the wire grid compared with Paxlovid- and vehicle-treated mice (example 9, pages 89 and 90; figures 14-17).
chemistry: Several compounds are specifically claimed, eg compound 22 (compound V; I) (table 1, page 69; claim 73, page 101). Seven compounds are excluded from the main claim (claim 1, page 92). A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 91). Sequences provided in source document.

title: Benzazepine derivatives, compositions, and methods for treating cognitive impairment
enhanced_title: New benzazepine derivatives are positive allosteric modulators of ?5-containing GABA A receptors - useful for treating CNS disorders and brain cancer.
novelty: New benzazepine derivatives, their salts, hydrates, solvates, polymorphs and isomers, compositions and combinations comprising them and their use for treating cognitive impairment associated with a central nervous system (CNS) disorder, eg autism, amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS), mild cognitive impairment (MCI), dementia, Alzheimer's disease, schizophrenia or bipolar disorder; and brain cancer, are claimed. The compounds are disclosed to be positive allosteric modulators of ?5-containing GABA A receptors.
advantage: The compounds are selectivity for GABA ?5 versus GABA ?3.
biology: In an in vivo test, effect of methyl 3,5-diphenylpyridazine-4-carboxylate (corresponding to (I)) in aged-impaired rats was studied using a RAM task. In the habituation phase, rats were familiarized to the maze for an 8-minute session on four consecutive days. Animals were then removed from the maze for 60 seconds, during which time the barriers on the maze were removed, thus allowing access to all eight arms. These rats were pretreated 30-40 minutes before daily trials with a one-time shot of the following five conditions: 1) vehicle control - 5% dimethyl sulfoxide, 25% polyethylene glycol 300 and 70% distilled water or methyl 3,5-diphenylpyridazine-4-carboxylate ( 0.3 or 3 mg/kg; ip). The average numbers of errors made by rats with the combined TB21007 (an ?5-containing GABAA R inverse agonist)/methyl 3,5-diphenylpyridazine-4-carboxylate treatment (0.3 mg/kg TB21007 with 3 mg/kg of the compound) was 2.88 ± 1.32, and was no different from rats treated with vehicle control (3.13 ± 1.17 average errors). Thus, the effect of methyl 3,5-diphenylpyridazine-4-carboxylate on spatial memory was a GABAA a5 receptor-dependent effect (example 167, pages 365-367; figure 1).
chemistry: Several compounds are specifically claimed, eg compound 6 (I) (example 95, pages 78 and 286; claim 22, page 398). A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 375).

After salt formation, the resulting mixture was filtered under nitrogen, washed with 50 mL of cyclohexane, and vacuum dried to yield crystalline form of (I) (19.5 g) (example 5, page 10).

After the set temperature was reached, the same solvent was slowly added to the suspension until the sample dissolved completely, or an anti-solvent (ie methyl tert-butyl ether) was slowly added to the clear solution. The solution was stirred at the set temperature for 30 mins, and the solution was then slowly cooled to room temperature and filtered (example 3, page 44). (I) are useful as p38 kinase inhibitor to produce medicine for preventing and/or treating p38 kinase-mediated diseases i.e. autoimmune diseases, inflammatory diseases, cardiovascular diseases, central nervous system diseases and cancer, preferably arthritis, psoriasis, systemic lupus erythematosus, diabetes, leukemia, lymphoma, atherosclerosis and Alzheimer's disease (all claimed) in animal including mammals, preferably mice, rats, other rodents, rabbits, dogs, cats, pigs, cows, sheep, horses or primates, preferably humans.
advantage: (I) have: good inhibitory effect on tumor necrosis factor alpha (TNF-? ) of human peripheral blood mononuclear cells; good in vivo pharmacokinetic property; good inhibitory activity against p38 MAPK/MK2; good selectivity for p38 MAPK/MK2; good in vitro liver microsome stability (human/mouse/rat); good in vivo pharmacokinetic properties; and good safety.
biology: P38 kinase inhibitor; P38 mitogen-activated protein kinase (MAPK)/mitogen-activated protein kinase-activated protein kinase 2 (MK2) inhibitor. Inhibitory activity of (I) was tested on p38 MAPK/MK2. The results showed that 3-chloro-4-((3,5-difluoropyridin-2-yl)methoxy)-1-((2S,3R,4S)-2-(3-((R)-1,2-dihydroxypropan-2-yl)-2,4-difluorophenyl)-3-fluoro-5-methylpyridin-4-yl)-6-methylpyridin-2(1H)-one exhibited IC 50 value of 0.09 nM.

The compounds are disclosed to act as TDP-43 and tau aggregation inhibitors.

advantage: No specific advantage is given.

title: Dual inhibitors for the treatment of Alzheimer's disease
enhanced_title: New heterocyclic compounds are dual inhibitors of sEH and glutaminyl cyclase - useful for treating alzheimer's disease.
novelty: New heterocyclic compounds, their salts and/or stereoisomers, compositions comprising them and their use for treating Alzheimer's disease, are claimed. The compounds act as dual inhibitors of sEH and glutaminyl cyclase.
advantage: The compounds have synergistic action on the contemporaneous neuroinflammation and amyloid pathology.
biology: An in vitro LDH toxicity assay was performed to evaluate the effect of the compounds using primary mouse cortical neurons prepared from timed pregnant wild-type C57BL/6JRccHsd mice, and neurotoxicity assessment of (I) (0.001 µM) showed primary cortical neuronal cell viability of 114% when compared to vehicle control (example 31, pages 28-30; table 4).
chemistry: Several compounds including 1-(3-ethyladamantan-1-yl)-3-[3-(5-methyl-1H-imidazol-1-yl)propyl]urea (I; compound Id) are specifically claimed (example 4, formula Id, page 9; claim 11, page 34). A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 32).

title: N-bridged diazocine analogues of carbamazepine to control neuronal activity with light and applications thereof
enhanced_title: New N-bridged diazocine analogues of carbamazepines - useful for treating eg epilepsy and neuropathic pain.
novelty: New N-bridged diazocine analogues of carbamazepines, their salts or stereoisomers, compositions comprising them and their use for treating peripheral nerve injury, painful polyneuropathy, postherpetic neuralgia, painful radiculopathy, trigeminal neuralgia, brachial plexus injury, phantom limb pain and epileptic focal seizure, are claimed.
advantage: The compounds are light activated, exhibit neuroinhibitory effects only localized at the region of illumination and have decreased severe adverse effects.
biology: An in vivo test was performed to evaluate the effect of the compounds using Sprague-Dawley rat pups and graphically depicted results showed that (I) generation of action potentials by hippocampal neurons was efficiently and repeatedly suppressed by blue light illumination and (II) (30 µM) decreased the frequency of the action potentials generation to 72 ± 5% when compared to control (frequency of the action potentials generation was to decreased to 28 ± 13%) (biological activity 2.1, pages 15-17; figure 2A).
chemistry: (Z)-Dibenzo[c,g][1,2,5]ltriazocine-11(12H)-carboxamide (I; compound of formula (II); LC-098) is the only compound specifically claimed (pages 14 and 15; claim 11, page 18). A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 17).

title: Sortilin modulators

novelty: New substituted heterocyclic amine compounds, their salts, solvates, hydrates, tautomers, optical isomers, N-oxides, and/or prodrugs and compositions comprising them are claimed. The compounds are stated to act as Sortilin receptor modulators.
advantage: The compounds exhibit an improved half-life in vivo compared to the compounds disclosed in EP22193792.3.
biology: An in vitro Neurotensin scintillation proximity assays (SPA) and the Human Sortilin GCI binding assays. Methanesulfonic acid form of (I) showed a low IC50 value of 0.44 µM in the SPA assay and a low KD value of 0.079 µM in the GCI assay (pages 45-47; table 1).
chemistry: Several compounds are specifically claimed, including (S)-2,5,5-trimethyl-2-(((5,6,7,8-tetrahydronaphthalen-2-yl)methyl)amino)hexanoic acid (I) (claim 9, page 71). Methanesulfonic acid form of (I) is disclosed as example 2, pages 37 and 39. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 65). Sequences provided in source document.

It is disclosed that the compounds act as sortilin modulators.
advantage: The compounds effectively modulate the activity of sortilin and are capable of crossing the blood brain barrier.
biology: An in vitro human sortilin GCI binding assay was performed to evaluate the affinity effect of the compounds and (I) exhibited KD value of 1.16E-0.7 M (pages 117-121; table 1).

title: Modulators of sortilin activity

novelty: New carboxylic acid compounds having adjacent quaternary carbon, their salts, solvates, hydrates, tautomers, optical isomers, N-oxides and/or prodrugs, and compositions comprising them are claimed, along with their use for the treatment or prevention of neurodegenerative (eg Alzheimer's disease), psychiatric (eg bipolar disorder), inflammatory, psoriasis, lysosomal storage disorders (eg Fabry disease), brain tumor, pain, diabetes mellitus, glaucoma, cardiovascular disease (eg atherosclerosis), hearing loss (eg tinnitus) or diseases characterized by misfolded tau. The compounds are stated to be sortilin ligands.
advantage: The compounds cross the blood brain barrier, exhibit improved binding to sortilin, and have an improved half-life in vivo.
biology: In an in vitro human sortilin Grating-Coupled Interferometry binding assay using human (6-His)sortilin, (I) showed a KD value of 0.393 ?M. Also,(I) showed an IC50 value in [3H]neurotensin scintillation proximity assay (pages 43-46; table 1).
chemistry: Several compounds including (S)-2-benzylamino-2,5,5-trimethylhexanoic acid (I) (example 2, page 36; claim 12, page 72) specifically claimed. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 66). Sequences provided in source document.

Also claimed are their use for treating mental health disorders, such as suicidal, depression, major depressive disorder, post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD), obsessive compulsive disorder, attention deficit hyperactivity disorder, eating disorder, anxiety, phobia disorders, and binge disorder; and a modulator of neurotransmission comprises activating a monoamine neurotransmitter receptor (a serotonin receptor (HTR (HTR1A, HTR1B, HTR2A, HTR2B, and HTR6)), a dopamine receptor, and a norepinephrine receptor) and/or modulating the uptake activity of a monoamine transporter (a serotonin transporter (SERT), a dopamine transporter (DAT), and a norepinephrine transporter (NET)).
advantage: The deuterated and fluorinated forms of compounds are stated to be more stable and soluble and enhanced metabolic stability, reduced toxicity, and other improvements on known compounds.
biology: A clinical study was performed to determine the effect of the test compounds, including (I) in patients with mental health disorders, such as PTSD and depression symptoms. Results showed that the test compounds are exhibited improvement in both PTSD and depression symptoms, without any notable lasting adverse effects (example 13, pages 120-122).
chemistry: Several deuterated and fluorinated empathogens, including illustrated compound (I) (claim 61, page 131) are specifically claimed. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 126).

advantage: No specific advantage is given.
biology: An in vitro test was performed on CF-HBE cells to assess the effect of (I) on CFTR-mediated chloride transport. VT and TEER were measured using TECC24, and chloride current (IEQ) was recorded after forskolin activation and CFTR inhibitor-172 addition. The AUC of the chloride current was calculated to evaluate test agent efficacy against the vehicle control. (I) showed an AUC value of >=12 @10 µM (example 30B, table 3; pages 359-361).
chemistry: Several compounds are specifically claimed including (R)-2-(4-(2-(5-((4,6-difluoro-1H-indol-5-yl)oxy)-2-fluorophenyl)-1H-imidazol-4-yl)-4-methylchroman-8-yl)acetic acid (compound 17) (I) (example 17, page 86; claim 45, page 373). A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 364).

A method of inhibiting a kinase using the compounds and an article of manufacture, are finally claimed. The compounds are disclosed to exhibit anti-proliferative effects by increasing PP2A activity in cells with suppressed or deficient phosphatase activity; and deactivate pro-growth and pro-survival kinases such as phospo-ERK and phospho-AKT and promote RNAPII promoter proximal pausing during transcription.

title: Phenylethanolamine N-methyltransferase (PNMT) inhibitors to regulate adrenaline responses

novelty: New substituted heterocyclic compounds, or its salts, combination and composition comprising them are claimed. The compounds are claimed to be useful for treating a central nervous disease, such as post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD), hypertension, myocardial infarction, Alzheimer's disease, Parkinson's disease, neuropathic pain, and hyperadrenergic condition. Also claimed a method of inhibiting phenylethanolamine N-methyltransferase (PNMT), wherein the PNMT is inhibited selectively over one or more targets selected from the group consisting of SAM-based protein arginine or lysine methyltransferases, DNA methyltransferases, nicotinamide methyltransferases, protein N- terminal methyltransferases and ?2-adrenoceptor.
advantage: The compounds effectively inhibit phenylethanolamine N-methyltransferase by mimicking enzymatic transition states, providing targeted and high-affinity enzyme inhibition.
biology: An in vitro test was performed to determine the effect of (I) against DNA methyltransferases, DNMT1 and DNMT3b. (I) gave IC50 values of 61 pM and 17 pM for DNMT1 and DNMT3b, demonstrating a specificity of 51,000 and 14,000 respectively for PNMT relative to the DNA methyltransferases (page 29).
chemistry: TSA Inhibitor 4 (I) (page 29; claim 14, page 35) is the only compound specifically claimed. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 33). One specific compound is excluded from claims (claim 1, page 34).

This white slurry was then centrifuged, the supernatant pipetted out and the wet residues dried under a flow of air overnight in the fume hood to obtain crystalline Form B of (I) (example 3, pages 62 and 63).

A health functional food for preventing or improving macular degeneration is also claimed. The treatment of amyloidosis is achieved by reducing deposition of amyloid beta (A?) and the treatment of macular degeneration is achieved by reducing degeneration of retinal pigment epithelial cells (RPE cells) is finally claimed.
advantage: The peptide offers superior stability and therapeutic efficacy against amyloidosis and macular degeneration compared to GV1001, requiring lower doses or fewer administrations. Additionally, it effectively reduces amyloid beta plaques, inflammation, ROS, and retinal cell degeneration.
biology: An in vivo test was performed to determine the effect of (I) in 5xFAD mice using Morris Water Maze Test. (I) improved spatial learning and memory in 5xFAD mice, as evidenced by reduced latency in finding the platform during training, increased zone crossings, and more time spent in the target quadrant during the probe test, demonstrating effects comparable to GV1001 (figure 4, page 6; example 4-1, page 15).

advantage: The forms have improved water solubility, enhanced bioavailability, better storage stability and better pharmacological profile compared to L-serine. The process is economical and scalable.
biology: An in vivo test was performed in valproic acid-induced autism model mice to determine the therapeutic effect of (I) on social behavior. Results showed that (I) significantly improved social novelty and sociability in the 3-chamber test, restoring behavior to control levels. The graphical illustration showed that the sociability interaction time for (I) is nearly 60s, which is 220 s for free base of (I) (experimental example 2, pages 13-14; figs 8A and 8B).
examples: To free base of (I) (4 kg), isopropyl alcohol (25 L) was added at RT, stirred and added hydrochloric acid (9.1 kg), stirred for 12 h at 10 °C, filtered, and washed with isopropyl alcohol (8 L) and vacuum-dried at 50 °C to obtain a stable, high-yield crystalline product of (I) with improved solubility and bioavailability (example 3, page 10).
chemistry: L-Serine hydrochloride is (I; formula 2) (page 2; claim 1, page 15).

novelty: New heterocyclic compounds, their salts, compositions comprising them and their use as TAK1 kinase inhibitors for treating Alzheimer's disease, head injury, concussions, traumatic brain injury are claimed.
advantage: The compounds exhibits high membrane permeability, and cell proliferation in live HepG2 cells.
biology: An in vivo test was conducted in P301S transgenic mice to evaluate the efficacy of (I)-dihydrochloride in reducing tau pathology associated with frontotemporal dementia. Mice were fed with HCL salt of (I) (~200 mg/kg/day) from 6.5 to 7.5 months of age, resulting in a 75% reduction in TAK1 phosphorylation (p < 0.05) and a 43% reduction in tau pathology (P = 0.08), as assessed by immunofluorescence microscopy (example 11, page 28)
chemistry: Two compounds including (I) (claim 4, page 30) are specifically claimed; however, hydrochloride salt of (I) is disclosed as 1-([3-fluoro-4-(1H-imidazol-1-yl)phenyl]methyl)-3-(4-[(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)methyl]-3-(trifluoromethyl)phenyl)urea (UK-5141) (page 20, table 1). A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 29).

title: Substituted heteroaryl analogs and uses thereof for the treatment of HIV-associated neurocognitive disorders
enhanced_title: New substituted heteroaryl derivatives are dopaminergic transporter and uptake - useful for treating HIV-associated neurocognitive disorder.
novelty: New substituted heteroaryl derivatives, their salts, compositions, combinations (antiretroviral agents), kits comprising them, and their use for treating a condition associated with dysregulated dopaminergic signaling, are claimed. Also claimed are methods for modifying dopaminergic signaling in a cell and treating a HAND (human immunodeficiency virus- (HIV-) associated neurocognitive disorder) in a subject. The compounds are disclosed to inhibit DAT ( DA transporter) binding and DA uptake.
advantage: No specific advantage is given.
biology: In an in vitro hDAT binding test, compounds were incubated with (I) showed an IC50 value of 0.52 ± 0.58 ?M (test example 1, pages 240-258; table 2).
chemistry: Several compounds are specifically claimed, eg 6-[4-[4-(4-fluorophenoxy)-1-piperidyl]-6-methyl-pyrimidin-2-yl]-7-methyl-imidazo[1,2-a]pyridine (compound 59a; I) (page 233; claim 114, page 304). One compound is excluded from (claim 70, page 289). Markush structures are presented (claim 1, page 262).

title: Agonists of TREM2 activity
enhanced_title: New carbonyl-substituted fused heteroaryl derivatives are agonists of TREM2 - useful for treating neurodegenerative disorders, eg multiple sclerosis.
novelty: New carbonyl-substituted fused heteroaryl derivatives, their salts, compositions and combination (tau targeting therapy or an amyloid-P targeting therapy) comprising them and their use as TREM2 receptor activators for treating neurodegenerative disorders, eg Alzheimer's disease, Parkinson's disease, frontotemporal dementia, demyelination disorder, multiple sclerosis, Huntington's disease, amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS), tauopathy disease, Nasu-Hakola disease, or adult-onset leukoencephalopathy with axonal spheroids and pigmented glia, are claimed.
advantage: The compounds exhibit good potency as agonists of TREM2.
biology: In an in vitro HEK293 test, TREM2 agonist potency was done using a HEK cell line expressing human TREM2 and DAP12 (HEK293T-hTREM2 cells). (I) showed an EC50 value of 9 nM (pages 261-275; table 5).
chemistry: Several compounds are specifically claimed, eg 4-(4-chloro-2-fluorophenyl)-2-((2R,4S)-2-(2-methylpyridin-4-yl)tetrahydro-2H-pyran-4-yl)-7,8-dihydropyrimido[5,4-d]pyrrolo[1,2-a]pyrimidin-10(6H)-one (I) (example 4-4, page 246; claim 35, page 295). Markush structures are presented (claim 1, page 276).

title: Agonists of TREM2 activity

title: Anilino-pyrazole derivatives, compositions and methods thereof
enhanced_title: Use of new and known anilino-pyrazole derivatives as CB2R agonists - useful for treating, eg anxiety and cardiac failure.
novelty: The use of new and known anilino-pyrazole derivatives, their salts or isotope derivatives, for treating a cannabinoid receptor type 2 (CB2R)-mediated disease or condition is also claimed, wherein the diseases are, eg chronic pain, osteoarthritis, anxiety, depression, addiction, heart failure, gingivitis, cutaneous T cell lymphoma, Tourette's syndrome and malaria and the compound is characterized by binding affinity to cyclin-dependent kinase 2 (CDK2) as measured by IC50 of greater than 100 nM. Compositions comprising the compounds are also claimed. A method for modulating the activity of CB2R by using the compounds is also claimed. The compounds are stated to act as CB2R agonists.
advantage: The compounds exhibit offer high potency, oral availability and selectivity over known CB2R modulators.
biology: An in vitro test was performed to evaluate the potency of CB2R modulators in cellular assays using cAMP-based detection. (I) showed an AC50 value of ?100 nM (page 274; table 1).
chemistry: Several compounds are exemplified, including (1R,3S)-3-{5-[(1,1-dioxo-2,3-dihydro-1?6-benzo[2,1-b]thiophen-7-yl)aniino]-2H-pyrazol-3-yI]cyclopentyl(prop-2-ylamino)methanoate (I) (example 35, page 189). No compounds are specifically claimed. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 278).

title: Phenylpiperazinamide derivatives modified with aliphatic amino acid fragments for the treatment of neurological diseases
enhanced_title: New phenylpiperazinamide derivatives modified with aliphatic amino acid fragments - useful for treating neurological diseases.
novelty: New substituted phenylpiperazinamide derivatives modified with aliphatic amino acid fragments are claimed. The compounds are claimed to be useful for treating neurological diseases, preferably epilepsy, neurological pain, neuropathic pain, inflammatory pain or migraine and is intended to induce an anti convulsive or analgesic effect.
advantage: The compounds exhibit broad anticonvulsant activity.
biology: AN in vivo test was performed to evaluate the analgesic effect of (I) using the formalin-induced pain model in mice. (I) showed significant inhibition of nociceptive responses at 25 and 50 mg/kg doses, with ED50 values of 34.3 mg/kg for acute pain (Phase I) and 22.0 mg/kg for inflammatory pain (Phase II) (figure 4, pages 25 and 26).
chemistry: Several compounds are specifically claimed, including (R,S)-N-(1-oxo-1-(4-(3-(trifluoromethoxy)phenyl)piperazin-1-yl)propan-2-yl)acetamide (I) (compound (R,S)-2; (wherein A=H, B=H, X=H, R1=OCF3, R2=CH3) (example 58, pages 6 and 49; claim 2, page 83). A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 83).

title: Organic compounds as muscarinic m4 receptor positive allosteric modulators (m4 pams)
enhanced_title: New substituted hetrocyclic compounds are muscarinic M4 receptor positive allosteric modulators - useful for treating psychiatric disorders.
novelty: New substituted hetrocyclic compounds or their salts, stereoisomer, or an isotopic form and compositions comprising them and their use as muscarinic M4 receptor positive allosteric modulators for treating psychiatric disorders eg anxiety, personality disorders, traumatic brain injury, obsessive-compulsive disorder, prion disease, attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder, schizophrenia, Rett syndrome, cognitive disorders and substance use disorders are claimed.
advantage: The compounds have potent affinity at M4, selectivity over muscarinic subtype receptors like M1, M2, M3 and M5, acceptable pharmacokinetic properties, good brain penetration, receptor occupancy and efficacy in animal models.
biology: An in vivo test was to evaluate the procognitive activity of test compounds using contextual fear conditioning in rats. (I) administered intraperitoneally at 30 mg/kg and 60 mg/kg, showed statistically significant reversal of deficits, confirming procognitive efficacy. Receptor occupancy for (I) was 45.5 ± 9.4% and 38.5 ± 2.4% at 30 mg/kg and 60 mg/kg respectively (examples 192 and 193, pages 135 and 136; tables 4 and 5).
chemistry: Several compounds including 1-(8-methyl-3,5,6,7-tetrahydro-1H-2,4-diaza-s-indacen-2-yl)-2-[1-(2-trifluoromethyl-pyridin-4-yl)azetidin-3-yl]ethanone (I) (example 2, page 68; claim 2, page 138) are specifically claimed. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 137).

title: Nitrogen-containing heteroaryl compounds for the treatment of chronic diseases
enhanced_title: New nitrogen-containing heteroaryl compounds are STAT6 inhibitors - useful for treating eg allergy, Alzheimer's disease, and atherosclerosis.
novelty: New nitrogen-containing heteroaryl compounds, their salts, compositions comprising them, and their use in treating (eg allergy, Alzheimer's disease, atherosclerosis, cancer, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, chronic pruritis, chronic urticaria, rhinosinusitis, systemic lupus erythematosus and vitiligo) are claimed. The compounds are disclsoed to act as STAT6 inhibitors.
advantage: No specific advantage is given.
biology: An in vitro test was performed to determine the the inhibitory potency of test compounds on CCL17 secretion in IL-13-stimulated human whole blood using a TR-FRET sandwich ELISA CCL17 assay Kit. (I) showed an IC50 of 0.083 ?M, whereas the most potent compound (example 183) showed an IC50 of 0.019 ?M (pages 164-169; table 2)
chemistry: Several compounds including N-((4-[(dimethylcarbamoyl)amino]phenyl)methyl)-5-[5-(dimethylcarbamoyl)pyridin-2-yl]-1-methyl-1H-indazole-3-carboxamide (I) (example 3, page 59; claim 30, page 180) are specifically claimed. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 173).

title: Compounds for treatment of a hyperkinetic movement disorder
enhanced_title: New substituted imidazo[2,1-b][1,3,4]thiadiazole derivatives are dopamine inhibitors - useful for treating hyperkinetic movement disorders.

advantage: The compound has good blood brain penetration efficacy, and specific to dopamine and does not affect serotonin and norepinephrine.
biology: In an in vivo experiment, intraperitoneally administration of (I) (1 mg/kg) showed significant reduction on dopamine levels without affecting 5-HT over control in male SD rats (pages 25 and 26; fig 1).
chemistry: Two compounds including 1-[[2-(methoxymethyl)-6-(trifluoromethyl)imidazo[2,1-b][1,3,4]thiadiazol-5-yl]methyl]-3-[(1R,2R)-2-(trifluoromethyl)cyclopropyl]-2H-pyrrol-5-one (I) (compound 2, table 1, page 21; claim 2, page 27) are specifically claimed.

chemistry: Several compounds including (3-chloro-2,4-dimethyl-5,7-dihydro-6H-pyrrolo[3,4-b]pyridin-6-yl)(3-(1-(difluoromethyl)-1H-pyrazol-3-yl)bicyclo[1.1.1]pentan-1-yl)methanone (I) (table 1, page 44; example 95, page 211; claim 49, page 239) are specifically claimed. advantage: The compounds exhibit strong GLP-1R agonist activity and good pharmacokinetic properties.

chemistry: Several compounds including RN-001 (I) (example 1, page 28; claim 13, page 94) are specifically claimed. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 87).

chemistry: Several compounds including compound 13 (I) (example 3, pages 155 and 159; claim 40, page 189) are specifically claimed. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 178).

Also claimed that the compound may further use in combination with erinacine A. The use of erinacine A (disclosed to derived from Hericium erinaceus mycelial), its salts or solvates for promoting wound healing is also claimed.
advantage: The compounds have good blood brain barrier crossing efficacy.

chemistry: Several compounds including erinacine A (I) (ErA, figure 1; claim 20, page 157) are specifically claimed for use. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 151).

title: Histone deacetylase inhibitors

novelty: New hydroxamic acid-based multivalent derivative compounds, compositions comprising them and their use in treating a disease involving deregulation of histone deacetylase (HDAC) include cancer (eg haematological and solid tumors), Crohn's disease, schizophrenia, HIV infection, Parkinson's and Alzheimer's diseases and Plasmodium falciparum and Schistosoma mansoni infection, are claimed. The compounds are stated to act as histone deacetylase inhibitors.
advantage: The compounds showed increased potency and efficacy compared to monovalent inhibitors.
biology: An in vitro test was conducted to determine the HDAC inhibition activity of the test compounds using a fluorescence-based assay. Results showed that (I) exhibited an EC50 value of 0.06 µM, indicated a 14-fold increase in potency compared to the monovalent inhibitor SAHA (EC50 of 0.85 µM) (Example 8, page 40-41).
chemistry: Several compounds including N1,N1'-(1,3-phenylene)bis(N8-hydroxyoctanediamide) (I) (given as MuH2324, page 24; claim 7, page 44) are specifically claimed. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 41). Sequences provided in source document.

advantage: The compounds show selective inhibition of MutS?.
biology: An in vitro test was conducted was conducted to evaluate the inhibitory activity of test compounds towards MutS?. (I) showed an IC50 value of ? 0.5 µM (Biological example A, page 146-151).

novelty: New deuterated benzene-ring-containing polycyclic derivatives, their salts, enantiomers and diastereomers, compositions comprising them and their use as Nav1.8 inhibitors for treating pain (inflammatory pain, cancer pain, neuropathic pain, musculoskeletal pain, intestinal pain and idiopathic pain), multiple sclerosis, incontinence or cardiac arrhythmia, are claimed.
advantage: The compounds have a novel structure; good blocking effect on NaV1.8; good selectivity for other subtypes of Nav (such as Nav1.1-1.9) and high safety.
biology: The analgesic efficacy of the compounds in the acetic acid writhing model in KM mice was tested. The results showed that compounds can inhibit the pain of mice caused by acetic acid and reduce the number of body twisting of mice. Naproxen (100 mg/kg) and (I) (30 mg/kg) showed number of twists to be 5.43 ± 5.62 and 7.25 ± 5.57, respectively (test example 6, page 37; table 7).
chemistry: Several compounds are specifically claimed, eg BX20-9-044 (I) (example 2, pages 14 and 17; claim 5, page 42). A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 40).

title: Novel indenone derivative and use thereof
enhanced_title: New indenone derivatives are inhibitors of ?-amyloid protein amd tau protein aggregation - useful for treating degenerative brain diseases.
novelty: New indanone derivatives and/or their salts, pharmaceutical and health functional food compositions comprising them, are claimed, along with their use for preventing or treating degenerative brain diseases (eg Alzheimer's disease, Huntington's disease, mild cognitive impairment, cerebral amyloid angiopathy, Down syndrome, Niemann-Pick disease, senile dementia, amyotrophic lateral sclerosis, Tourette's syndrome and frontotemporal dementia) by inhibiting aggregation of amyloid-? protein and tau protein, decomposing these proteins, or inhibiting phosphorylation of tau protein.
advantage: The compounds exhibit significantly superior effects in decomposing amyloid-? and/or tau aggregate.
biology: An in vitro amyloid-? and tau aggregation dissociation test was performed by thioflavin-T analysis using amyloid-? and tau solutions prepared by dissolving A? 1-42 or tau-RD3 (tau repeat domain 3) monomers in DMSO. Several compounds including (I) exhibited strong disintegration effects on amyloid-? and tau aggregates with EC50 values of ?20 ?M (example 1, pages 161-164; table 2).
chemistry: Several compounds including 6-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-2,3-diphenyl-1H-inden-1-one (I) (manufacturing example 13, compound 13, page 56; claim 6, page 170) are specifically claimed. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 166).

title: Arabinose derivative compound, and composition comprising same for preventing or treating degenerative brain diseases
enhanced_title: New GPCR T1R3-targeting arabinose derivatives - useful for treating degenerative brain diseases.
novelty: New arabinose derivatives, their salts, isomers, hydrates, solvates and/or prodrugs, and pharmaceutical and food compositions comprising them are claimed, along with their use for brain nerve regeneration and protection by targeting GPCR T1R3 for the prevention or treatment of degenerative brain diseases eg stroke, dementia, Parkinson's disease, epilepsy, brain tumor, brain edema, paralysis, meningitis, meningioma, and Gerstmann syndrome.
advantage: The compounds exhibit improved cognitive function and enhanced long-term memory.
biology: An in vivo test was performed in scopolamine-induced cognitive impairment ICR mice model to determine the cognitive-enhancing effects of the test compounds. The compounds were orally administered at 50 mg/kg for 7 days, and donepezil (5 mg/kg) was used as a positive control. Cognitive function recovery in (I) treated group was 59% in the Y-maze experiment for measuring short-term memory through spontaneous alternation behavior and 91% in passive avoidance test for measuring memory retention compared to normal animals, which was equal to or greater than the positive control donepezil (35 and 94%, respectively) (experimental example 2, pages 89-91; table 3).
chemistry: Several compounds including (2R,3R,4S,5S)-2-(4-(4-methoxybenzyl)phenoxy)tetrahydro-2H-pyran-3,4,5-triol (I) (example 3, page 44; claim 17, page 96) are specifically claimed. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 94).

advantage: The compound exhibits potent LRRK2 kinase inhibition with improved crystalline stability and physicochemical properties.
biology: An in vitro MSD assay was conducted to determine the potency of test compounds. (I) showed an IC50 value of 17 nM against wild-type LRRK2 (page 44-45).

Mice treated with (I) (20 mg/kg) 30 minutes before and 10 hours after LPS injection showed significantly prolonged survival compared to the model group (example 5, page 38, figure 3)
chemistry: Several compounds including RGW-1 (I) (example 1, page 11; claim 10, page 44) are specifically claimed. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 39).

The compounds are stated to act as MAO-B selective inhibitors.
advantage: The compounds effectively improves cognitive function and have selective inhibitory activity on MAO-B.
biology: An in vivo test was performed to assess the weight-reducing effect of a test compound (10 mg/kg/day, oral) in DIO male mice (45-55 g, n=6). By day 24, mice treated with the test compound showed a body weight reduction of approximately -11.5%, compared to -6% with KDS2010 (10 mg/kg). Despite the weight loss, average daily food intake remained steady at around 3.5 g/day across all groups (test example 3, pages 45 and 46; figures 1-3),
chemistry: Several compounds are specifically claimed, including compound 1 (I) (example 1, page 26; claim 8, page 59). A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 48).

title: Novel compounds for tau imaging

novelty: Novel benzo[4,5]imidazo[1,2-a]pyrimidine derivatives, their salts, compositions comprising them and their use for diagnostic imaging, are claimed. Also claimed is a method of imaging aggregated tau in a mammal by introducing into a mammal a detectable quantity of the compound or composition, for detecting neurodegenerative disorders such as progressive supranuclear palsy, cortical basal degeneration, Pick's disease, atypical Alzheimer's disease (AD), chronic traumatic encephalopathy, and frontotemporal dementia.
advantage: The compounds are selective tau imaging agents that enhance PET imaging clarity, enabling early diagnosis and differentiation of AD and non-AD tauopathies.
biology: An autoradiography test was performed to determine the binding of (I) to non-AD tau aggregates in human brain tissues diagnosed with progressive supranuclear palsy (PSP) and corticobasal degeneration (CBD) using autoradiography. Results showed ARG positivity in tau-rich regions, indicated selective binding to non-AD tau aggregates, also showed strong binding to AD tau, suggested its utility in detecting both AD and non-AD tauopathies (example 23, pages 48-49; Figure 1-2).

It is disclosed that the invention provides highly selective antagonists predicted to reduce off-target side effects and extrapyramidal symptoms.
advantage: The compounds exhibit extreme D2 receptor selectivity, improved metabolic stability, high brain penetration, and reduced risk of extrapyramidal side effects compared to existing antipsychotics.
biology: An in vivo test was performed to evaluate the antipsychotic potential of (I) in a mouse PPI model of amphetamine-induced sensorimotor gating deficit. Graphical results showed that amphetamine reduced pre-pulse inhibition (PPI) of acoustic startle to nearly 20-35%, while (I) (0.05-0.5 mg/kg, i.p.) increased PPI to nearly 45-70% across 4, 8, and 12 dB prepulses, approaching vehicle control levels (page 26; figure 13).

The compounds are stated to act as MAO B inhibitors.
advantage: The compounds exhibit highly selective and enhanced MAO-B inhibition, enabling effective diagnosis, imaging, and treatment of MAO-B-overexpressing neurological diseases.
biology: An in vitro test was performed to evaluate MAO-B selective inhibitory activity of the test compounds using human monoamine oxidase-B, with THK5351 as a comparator using an Amplex Red assay. (I) exhibited a MAO-B IC50 of 0.0063 ?M with MAO-A IC50 >100 ?M, corresponding to a selectivity of 15,873 over MAO-A and superior potency compared with THK5351 (example 1, pages 17 and 18).

Its anti-allodynic effect was blocked by the peripheral CB1 antagonist AM6545, but not by the CB2 antagonist AM630, confirming CB1 selective peripheral mechanism. also, (I) showed minimal tolerance over 9?day repeated dosing and triggers tetrad behaviors (catalepsy, hypothermia, antinociception) only at ?100× analgesic dose (table S3; pages 101 and 102).
