enhanced_title: New substituted heterocyclic compounds are glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide antagonists - useful for treating eg type 1 diabetes mellitus, chronic kidney disease, osteoarthritis, depression and cardiovascular disease.
novelty: New substituted heterocyclic compounds or its salt or stereoisomer and composition comprising them are claimed. The compounds are claimed to be useful for antagonizing glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide receptor (GIPR) and treating or preventing GIPR-related diseases such as meta2bolic disorders, obesity, dyslipidemia, cardiovascular and renal diseases, neurological and inflammatory disorders, gastrointestinal disorders, PCOS, osteoarthritis, and Alzheimer's disease.
advantage: No specific advantage is given.
biology: A pharmacokinetic study was conducted in male C57BL/6J mice following single intravenous (1 mpk) or oral gavage (5 mpk) administration to evaluate in vivo exposure and bioavailability of the disclosed compounds using LC/MS/MS plasma analysis. Compound 106 showed potent pharmacokinetic properties with IV T1/2 of 6.3 h, AUC0-t of 14954 ng·h/mL, clearance of 1.0 mL/min/kg, and oral T1/2 of 6.7 h, Cmax of 5901 ng/mL, AUC0-t of 66511 ng·h/mL, and oral bioavailability (F) of 89% (example 3, table 14, pages 211-212).
chemistry: Several compounds are specifically claimed including compound 106 (I) (table 1, page 111; claim 108, page 246). A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 214).

title: Fused ring compound, pharmaceutical composition comprising same, and use thereof
enhanced_title: New fused ring compounds are potassium channel inhibitors - useful for treating CNS diseases.
novelty: New fused ring compounds, their salts, esters, tautomers, stereoisomers, compositions comprising them, and their use as potassium channel (disclosed as KCNQ2 and KCNQ2/3) modulators or as analgesia for treating CNS diseases including epilepsy, depression, amyotrophic lateral sclerosis, cognitive impairment, neuropathic pain, multiple sclerosis, Parkinson's disease, dystonia, anxiety, and bipolar disorder, are claimed.
advantage: The compounds have stronger antiepileptic effect over retigabine.
biology: In an in vivo experiment, (I) (2.5 mpk) showed 100% protection rate in mice with epilepsy, whereas retigabine (30 mpk) showed 62.5% protection rate in the mice (test example 3, page 38; table 4).
chemistry: Several compounds including N-(7-cyclopropyl-1-(prop-2-yn-1-yl)-1H-indazol-3-yl)-3,3-dimethylbutanamide (I) (compound 5, example 5, page 23; claim 4, page 42) are specifically claimed. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 40).

title: Pyrazolone compounds for use in treatment of neurodegenerative disease
enhanced_title: Use of substituted pyrazolone compounds as amyloid-? aggregation inhibitors - for treating Alzheimer's disease in subject.
novelty: The use of substituted pyrazolone compounds or their salts, hydrates, solvates or compositions as amyloid-? aggregation inhibitors, for treating Alzheimer's disease (AD) in a subject, is claimed. A method of inhibiting amyloid-aggregation and/or reducing amyloid-aggregation-induced toxicity, substituted pyrazolone compounds or their salts, hydrates, solvates and an oral composition comprising the compounds, are also claimed.
advantage: The compounds are capable of permeating the BBB and designed to synergistically target A? and modulate the activity of other key biological targets behind AD pathological processes may provide therapies that more efficiently meet the patients needs, reduce the effect of reactive species, protect nerve cells from oxidative stress toxicity and reduce neurodegeneration for AD patients.
biology: An in vivo pharmacokinetic study was conducted to measure pharmacokinetic profiles of (I) in mice. Results showed that mice intravenously administered with (I) (5 mg/kg) exhibited AUClast, t1/2, CL, Vinf and C0/Cmax values of 294.55 hr*ng/mL, 1.15 h, 275.06 mL/min/kg, 9.98 L/kg and 1277.49 ng/mL respectively (page 32; table 3).
chemistry: Seven compounds including compound 1 (I) (page 9; claim 11, page 49) are specifically claimed for use in the composition. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 48).

title: Small molecule inhibitors of DYRK1A/CLK and uses thereof
enhanced_title: New imidazo-pyridine compounds are DYRK1A, DYRK1B, and CLK-1 inhibitors - useful for treating Alzheimer's disease, diabetes, autoimmune diseases.
novelty: New heterocyclic compounds, their salts, prodrugs, and/or solvates, compositions comprising them and their use as inhibitors of DYRK1A, DYRK1B, and Clk-1 for the treatment of cognitive disorders, Huntington's disease, Alzheimer's disease, Down syndrome, diabetes, autoimmune diseases, inflammatory disorders, cancer, glioblastoma, prostate cancer and are claimed.
advantage: The compounds are potent in their action.
biology: An in vitro test was conducted to determine the dissociation constant of the test compounds. (I) showed a KD value of < 0.1nM (page 13, table 1).
chemistry: Several compounds including compound 1 (I) (pages 50 and 56, table 1) are exemplified. No compounds are specifically claimed. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 107)

title: Inhibitors of glycine transporter 2 for the treatment of neuropathic pain
enhanced_title: New heterocyclic derivatives are GlyT2 inhibitors - useful for treating neuropathic pain.
novelty: New heterocyclic derivatives, their salts, compositions comprising them, and their use as GlyT2 inhibitors, are claimed. The compounds are dsiclosed to be useful for treating neuropathic pain.
advantage: The compounds are competitive and reversible GlyT2 inhibitors.
biology: In an in vivo test,(I)'s pharmacokinetics were evaluated in male C57BL/6 mice, with results reported as mean ± standard deviation at the 30-minute time point following administration in a vehicle. The study indicated that (I) achieved measurable systemic exposure within 30 minutes, demonstrating rapid absorption and distribution in vivo under the tested conditions. (I) (2 mg/kg; iv dose) showed CL of 3844 (mL/min/kg) and at a po dose of 50 mg/kg, showed Cmax of 2701.6 (ng/mL), with 27.6% of bioavailability (example 123, pages 110-114; table 6).
chemistry: Several compounds are exemplified, eg (1-benzoyl-N-((4-(dimethylamino)tetrahydro-2H-pyran-4-yl)methyl)indoline-5-carboxamide (compound 47a (RPI-GLYT2-82) (I) (examples 48 and 51, pages 13 and 69). No compounds are specifically claimed. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 118).

title: Polycyclic compound, preparation method therefor, and use thereof
enhanced_title: New polycyclic derivatives are PCSK9 inhibitors - useful for treating, eg dyslipidemia, coronary heart disease, metabolic syndrome, stroke, vascular dementia, inflammation and thrombosis.
novelty: New polycyclic derivatives, their stereoisomers, tautomers, deuterated derivatives, crystal forms, salts, hydrates, solvates, or prodrugs, compositions comprising them, and their use for treating, eg dyslipidemia, hypercholesterolemia, hypertriglyceridemia, coronary heart disease, metabolic syndrome, diabetic complications, atherosclerosis, stroke, vascular dementia, inflammation and thrombosis, are claimed. The compounds are disclosed to be PCSK9 inhibitors.
advantage: No specific advantage is given.
biology: In an in vitro PCSK9 protein binding assay, compounds were incubated with PCSK9 protein. Several compounds, including (I) showed a KD vale of ?100 nM (assay A, pages 62-66; table 15).
chemistry: Several compounds are specifically claimed, eg 6'-(((1S,3S)-3-((7-acetyl-5,6,7,8-tetrahydropyridino[3,4-d]pyrimidin-2-yl)amino)cyclopentyl)amino)-2H-[1,3'-bipyridine]-2-one (I) (example 2, pages 28 and 29; claim 8, page 71). A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 67).

title: Nitrogen-containing heterocyclic compound and use thereof
enhanced_title: New nitrogen-containing heterocyclic analogs are PCSK9 antagonists - useful for treating cardiovascular diseases, liver diseases, infectious and autoimmune diseases, neurocognitive disorders, and cancer.
novelty: New nitrogen-containing heterocyclic compounds, their stereoisomers, tautomers, or mixtures and salts, compositions comprising them, and their use for treating cardiovascular diseases, liver diseases, infectious and autoimmune diseases, neurocognitive disorders, and cancer, are claimed. A method for lowering LDL includes administering to an individual, using the compounds, is also claimed. The compounds are disclosed to be PCSK9 antagonists.
advantage: The compounds are selective, highly potent PCSK9 inhibitors with excellent oral bioavailability, favorable in vivo pharmacokinetics.
biology: In an in vivo test, (I) (1.5 mg/kg administered via gavage) showed T1/2 (h), Tmax (in h), Cmax (ng/mL) and AUClast (h.ng/mL) to be 23.7, 4.0, 4157, and 108285, respectively, in cynomolgus monkeys (experiment example 5, pages 61 and 62; table 5).
chemistry: Several compounds including 2-(7-(((1S,3S)-3-((5-(difluoromethoxy)pyrimidin-2-yl)amino)cyclopentyl)amino)furano[2,3-c]pyridin-4-yl)pyridazin-3(2H)-one (compound 38; I) (example 38, page 34; claim 21, page 76) are specifically claimed. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 63).

title: Compounds, compositions, and methods
enhanced_title: New fused heterocyclic compounds are calcitonin and amylin receptors stimulators - useful for treating, eg metabolic disorders, pain, cardiovascular diseases and neurodegenerative diseases.
novelty: New fused heterocyclic compounds, their salts, stereoisomers, solvates, compositions and combinations comprising them, and their use for treating calcitonin and amylin receptors activity including metabolic disorders, pain, cardiovascular diseases, neurodegenerative diseases, osteoporosis, hypercalcemia, non-alcoholic fatty liver disease, Alzheimer's disease, insulin dependent diabetes and gastrointestinal disorders, are claimed. The compounds are disclosed to act as calcitonin and amylin receptors stimulators.
advantage: No specific advantage is given.
biology: Amylin receptor (AMY3) and calcitonin receptor (CTR) cAMP assays were conducted in COS-7 cells stably expressing human CTR/RAMP3 or CTR alone to assess Gs-mediated cAMP activity using serially diluted compounds and fluorescence detection at 665/615 nm. Compound 346 showed potent activity with EC50 values of 4.66 nM against AMY3 and 0.031 nM against CTR (pages183-186; table 3).
chemistry: Several compounds including compound 154 (I) (table 1, pages 60 and 181; claim 19, page 193) are specifically claimed. A Markush structure is presented (claim 1, page 187).
